## πραιτώριον (id: 004134000000000)

πραιτώριον
==========

πραιτώριον (G4232)

noun \| \-ου

7\.7 · Constructions · Buildings

palace, fortress the dwelling of a king or ruler; usually larger and more elaborate than the dwelling of an average person There are differences of opinion as to the particular πραιτώριον mentioned in Jerusalem. It may have been either the palace of Herod in the western part of the city or the fortress Antonia northwest of the Temple area. The πραιτώριον in Caesarea was the palace built by Herod the Great (see [ACT 23:35](https://ref.ly/John23:35)).  
In the Gospels it is satisfactory to translate πραιτώριον\[a] as ‘the palace where the governor lived’ or ‘the large dwelling where the ruler lived.’ In a number of languages a term for ‘palace’ often suggests a military fortification, and this would accordingly be quite appropriate for NT usage.

[MAT 27:27](https://ref.ly/Matt27:27) · [MRK 15:16](https://ref.ly/Matt15:16) · [JHN 18:28](https://ref.ly/Luke18:28) · [JHN 18:28](https://ref.ly/Luke18:28) · [JHN 18:33](https://ref.ly/Luke18:33) · [JHN 19:9](https://ref.ly/Luke19:9) · [ACT 23:35](https://ref.ly/John23:35)

55\.12 · Military Activities · Army

group of soldiers, palace guard a detachment of soldiers serving as the palace guard

[PHP 1:13](https://ref.ly/Eph1:13)

* **Associated Passages:** Matthew 27:27; Mark 15:16; John 18:28; John 18:33; John 19:9; Acts 23:35; Philippians 1:13

## πρᾶξις (id: 004136000000000)

πρᾶξις
======

πρᾶξις (G4234)

noun \| \-εως \| πράσσω

42\.8 · Perform, Do · Do, Perform

to do, to carry out, to perform, deed to carry out some activity (with possible focus upon the procedures involved)

[MAT 16:27](https://ref.ly/Matt16:27) · [LUK 23:51](https://ref.ly/Mark23:51) · [ACT 19:18](https://ref.ly/John19:18) · [ROM 8:13](https://ref.ly/Acts8:13) · [COL 3:9](https://ref.ly/Phil3:9)

42\.5 · Perform, Do · Function

function, task a function, implying sustained activity and/or responsibility In some languages one may express the meaning of this phrase in [ROM 12:4](https://ref.ly/Acts12:4) as ‘all these parts help in different ways’ or ‘all these parts have different work to do.’

[ROM 12:4](https://ref.ly/Acts12:4)

## πρασιά (id: 004137000000000)

πρασιά
======

πρασιά (G4237)

noun \| \-ᾶς \| πράσσω

11\.6 · Groups and Classes of Persons and Members of Such Groups and Classes · General

group (a figurative extension of meaning of πρασιά ‘garden plot,’ not occurring in the NT) a group of persons arranged in an orderly fashion A comparison of πρασιαὶ πρασιαί in [MRK 6:40](https://ref.ly/Matt6:40) with συμπόσια συμπόσια (see [11\.5](#11.5)) in [MRK 6:39](https://ref.ly/Matt6:39) indicates clearly that the reference in both instances is essentially the same, but in [MRK 6:39](https://ref.ly/Matt6:39) συμπόσια συμπόσια points to the grouping of people with the intent of eating, while in [MRK 6:40](https://ref.ly/Matt6:40) πρασιαὶ πρασιαί marks the orderly arrangement of the persons. One may therefore translate [MRK 6:40](https://ref.ly/Matt6:40) as ‘the people sat down in orderly groups of a hundred and of fifty’ or ‘the people sat down in rows by groups of a hundred and groups of fifty.’

[MRK 6:40](https://ref.ly/Matt6:40) · [MRK 6:40](https://ref.ly/Matt6:40)

* **Associated Passages:** Mark 6:40

======

πράσσω (G4238)

pf. πέπραχα · pf.pass. πέπραγμαι \| διαπραγματεύομαι · πρᾶγμα · πράκτωρ · πρᾶξις · πρασιά · χρυσόπρασος

42\.8 · Perform, Do · Do, Perform

to do, to carry out, to perform, deed to carry out some activity (with possible focus upon the procedures involved)

[LUK 22:23](https://ref.ly/Mark22:23) · [LUK 23:15](https://ref.ly/Mark23:15) · [LUK 23:41](https://ref.ly/Mark23:41) · [LUK 23:41](https://ref.ly/Mark23:41) · [JHN 3:20](https://ref.ly/Luke3:20) · [JHN 5:29](https://ref.ly/Luke5:29) · [ACT 3:17](https://ref.ly/John3:17) · [ACT 5:35](https://ref.ly/John5:35) · [ACT 15:29](https://ref.ly/John15:29) · [ACT 16:28](https://ref.ly/John16:28) · [ACT 17:7](https://ref.ly/John17:7) · [ACT 19:19](https://ref.ly/John19:19) · [ACT 19:36](https://ref.ly/John19:36) · [ACT 25:11](https://ref.ly/John25:11) · [ACT 25:25](https://ref.ly/John25:25) · [ACT 26:9](https://ref.ly/John26:9) · [ACT 26:20](https://ref.ly/John26:20) · [ACT 26:26](https://ref.ly/John26:26) · [ACT 26:31](https://ref.ly/John26:31) · [ROM 1:32](https://ref.ly/Acts1:32) · [ROM 1:32](https://ref.ly/Acts1:32) · [ROM 2:1](https://ref.ly/Acts2:1) · [ROM 2:2](https://ref.ly/Acts2:2) · [ROM 2:3](https://ref.ly/Acts2:3) · [ROM 2:25](https://ref.ly/Acts2:25) · [ROM 7:15](https://ref.ly/Acts7:15) · [ROM 7:19](https://ref.ly/Acts7:19) · [ROM 9:11](https://ref.ly/Acts9:11) · [ROM 13:4](https://ref.ly/Acts13:4) · [1CO 9:17](https://ref.ly/Rom9:17) · [2CO 5:10](https://ref.ly/1Cor5:10) · [2CO 12:21](https://ref.ly/1Cor12:21) · [GAL 5:21](https://ref.ly/2Cor5:21) · [PHP 4:9](https://ref.ly/Eph4:9) · [1TH 4:11](https://ref.ly/Col4:11)

57\.65 · Possess, Transfer, Exchange · Take, Obtain, Gain, Lose

to receive (interest), to collect (taxes) to collect what is due (normally in terms of taxes and interest), with the possible implication of extortion (as in [LUK 3:13](https://ref.ly/Mark3:13))

[LUK 3:13](https://ref.ly/Mark3:13) · [LUK 19:23](https://ref.ly/Mark19:23)

90\.76 · Case · Experiencer

to experience, to fare to experience events and to also engage in them1

[EPH 6:21](https://ref.ly/Gal6:21)

1 This meaning of πράσσω is particularly complex because it certainly is not restricted merely to what one is actually overtly engaged in doing. At the same time one must recognize that the meaning is not merely to undergo experiences. It is this particularly complex aspect of the meaning of πράσσω which is perhaps best reflected in ‘how I am faring’ or ‘how I am making out.’

* **Associated Passages:** Luke 22:23; Luke 23:15; Luke 23:41; John 3:20; John 5:29; Acts 3:17; Acts 5:35; Acts 15:29; Acts 16:28; Acts 17:7; Acts 19:19; Acts 19:36; Acts 25:11; Acts 25:25; Acts 26:9; Acts 26:20; Acts 26:26; Acts 26:31; Romans 1:32; Romans 2:1; Romans 2:2; Romans 2:3; Romans 2:25; Romans 7:15; Romans 7:19; Romans 9:11; Romans 13:4; 1 Corinthians 9:17; 2 Corinthians 5:10; 2 Corinthians 12:21; Galatians 5:21; Philippians 4:9; 1 Thessalonians 4:11; Luke 3:13; Luke 19:23; Ephesians 6:21


## πραϋπαθία (id: 004139000000000)

πραϋπαθία
=========

πραϋπαθία (G4236a)

noun \| \-ας \| πραΰς \+ πάσχω

88\.59 · Moral and Ethical Qualities and Related Behavior · Gentleness, Mildness

gentleness, meekness, mildness gentleness of attitude and behavior, in contrast with harshness in one’s dealings with others In a number of languages ‘gentleness’ is often expressed as a negation of harshness, so that ‘gentleness’ may often by rendered as ‘not being harsh with people,’ but gentleness may also be expressed in some instances in an idiomatic manner, for example, ‘always speaking softly to’ or ‘not raising one’s voice.’

[1TI 6:11](https://ref.ly/2Thess6:11)

* **Associated Passages:** 1 Timothy 6:11

## πρεσβεία (id: 004143000000000)

πρεσβεία
========

πρεσβεία (G4242)

noun \| \-ας \| πρεσβεύω

37\.87 · Control, Rule · Rule, Govern

representative, ambassador a person who has been given authority to communicate or to act on behalf of a ruler1

[LUK 14:32](https://ref.ly/Mark14:32) · [LUK 19:14](https://ref.ly/Mark19:14)

1 It is possible to assign the meanings of πρεσβεία ([37\.87](#37.87)) and πρεσβεύω ([37\.88](#37.88)) to the domain of Communication (33\), since communication is an important factor in the function of being a representative. However, the delegated authority and responsibility seem to be more in focus.

## πρεσβεύω (id: 004144000000000)

πρεσβεύω
========

πρεσβεύω (G4243)

πρεσβεία · πρεσβυτέριον · πρεσβύτερος · πρεσβύτης · πρεσβῦτις · συμπρεσβύτερος

37\.88 · Control, Rule · Rule, Govern

to be a representative of, to be an ambassador of (derivative of πρεσβεία ‘representative,’ [37\.87](#37.87)) to function as a representative of a ruling authority1 It may be possible to render ὑπὲρ Χριστοῦ οὖν πρεσβεύομεν in [2CO 5:20](https://ref.ly/1Cor5:20) as ‘we serve as those who have been delegated by Christ’ or ‘our work has been specially assigned by Christ.’

[2CO 5:20](https://ref.ly/1Cor5:20) · [EPH 6:20](https://ref.ly/Gal6:20)

1 It is possible to assign the meanings of πρεσβεία ([37\.87](#37.87)) and πρεσβεύω ([37\.88](#37.88)) to the domain of Communication (33\), since communication is an important factor in the function of being a representative. However, the delegated authority and responsibility seem to be more in focus.

* **Associated Passages:** 2 Corinthians 5:20; Ephesians 6:20


## πρεσβυτέριον (id: 004145000000000)

πρεσβυτέριον
============

πρεσβυτέριον (G4244)

noun \| \-ου \| πρεσβεύω

11\.83 · Groups and Classes of Persons and Members of Such Groups and Classes · Socio\-Political

Sanhedrin, high council of the Jews the highest council of the Jews (see also συνέδριον\[b] ‘Sanhedrin,’ the council of the Jews, [11\.80](#11.80)) but with the implication of the maturity and relative advanced age of those constituting the membership of such a council1

[LUK 22:66](https://ref.ly/Mark22:66) · [ACT 22:5](https://ref.ly/John22:5)

11\.84 · Groups and Classes of Persons and Members of Such Groups and Classes · Socio\-Political

group of elders, elders a council of elders, with emphasis upon maturity of judgment more than mere age

[1TI 4:14](https://ref.ly/2Thess4:14)

1 Though γερουσία and πρεσβυτέριον\[a] refer to the same council of the Jews as the term συνέδριον\[b] ([11\.80](#11.80)), this does not mean that the three terms are completely identical in meaning. The focus upon maturity of age and judgment as well as presumed social standing are more significant factors in γερουσία and πρεσβυτέριον\[a] than in συνέδριον\[b].

* **Associated Passages:** Luke 22:66; Acts 22:5; 1 Timothy 4:14

noun \| \-ου \| πρεσβεύω

old man, old woman an adult male or female advanced in years1

53\.77 · Religious Activities · Roles and Functions

elder a person of responsibility and authority in matters of socio\-religious concerns, both in Jewish and Christian societies In some languages πρεσβύτερος\[b] is best rendered as ‘older leaders,’ but in other languages the more appropriate term would be the equivalent of ‘counselor,’ since it would be assumed that counselors would be older than the average person in a group as well as having authority to lead and direct activities.

65\.54 · Value · Important, Unimportant

more important than, of greatest importance, above marker of primary importance

[COL 1:17](https://ref.ly/Phil1:17) · [JAS 5:12](https://ref.ly/Heb5:12) · [1PE 4:8](https://ref.ly/Jas4:8)

67\.58 · Time · A Point of Time with Reference to Other Points of Time: Before, Long Ago, Now, At the Same Time, When, About, After

**Collocation:** πρὸ θυρῶν

soon, very soon (an idiom, literally: before the doors) a point of time subsequent to another point of time and indicating imminence, that is to say, the subsequent event is regarded as almost begun

[JAS 5:9](https://ref.ly/Heb5:9)

67\.133 · Time · Duration of Time with Reference to Some Point of Time: Until, Delay, Still, From

**Collocation:** πρὸ παντὸς τοῦ αἰῶνος

since all time, from all ages past, from the beginning of time an exceedingly long period of time from an assumed beginning up to the present

[2TI 1:9](https://ref.ly/1Tim1:9) · [JUD 1:25](https://ref.ly/3John1:25)

67\.19 · Time · A Point of Time with Reference to Other Points of Time: Before, Long Ago, Now, At the Same Time, When, About, After

**Collocation:** πρὸ προσώπου

before, previous (an idiom, literally: before the face, equivalent in meaning to πρό\[b] ‘before,’ [67\.17](#67.17), but a somewhat more elaborate phrase rhetorically) a point of time, possibly only a short time before another point of time

of sheep (derivative of πρόβατον ‘sheep,’ [4\.22](#4.22)) pertaining to sheep The phrase ἐπὶ τῇ προβατικῇ is an elliptical expression in which the noun for ‘gate’ (which is feminine in gender) is omitted. The phrase ‘at the Sheep Gate’ may be rendered in some languages as ‘close to the gate of the city through which sheep entered’ or ‘... were driven’ or ‘... were led.’

## πρόβατον (id: 004163000000000)

πρόβατον
========

πρόβατον (G4263)

noun \| \-ου \| βαίνω (go, walk)

4\.22 · Animals · Animals

sheep herbivorous domestic mammal with four legs; Ovis ladicaudata \- fat\-tailed sheep; used for sacrifice, source of meat, milk, hides, wool, and horns Since the distribution of sheep throughout the world is somewhat less than that of goats, there may be more difficulties involved in finding an adequate term. In some instances terms for sheep are actually based upon a term for goat. For example, among Eskimos goats are well known because there are wild goats in the mountains, and sheep are accordingly called in some dialects ‘woolly goats’ or ‘goats that have wool.’ Among the Maya in Yucatan sheep were introduced by the Spanish and were first described as being ‘cotton deer,’ and the name still persists.1 In some areas where sheep are not known at all, the meat of sheep is known, and accordingly sheep may be called by the name of the meat, for example, ‘mutton animals.’ But in most places where sheep are not indigenous, there is nevertheless some term for them, usually based upon a borrowing from a dominant language.  
For the translator, however, there are often problems involving the connotations of the term for sheep and for related expressions concerning their behavior and the ways in which they are ‘led.’ As already noted under the discussion of goats ([4\.19](#4.19)), sheep are frequently less prized than goats, and therefore it may seem strange in biblical texts to find sheep being given a preferential rating. What may appear even more difficult to understand is the suggestion that sheep are led by a shepherd rather than being driven. This fact may require some type of marginal note to accompany the text if people are to understand the basis for a number of similes and metaphors of the Scriptures. Furthermore, if the experience which people have of sheep is only their knowledge about wild sheep, then obviously references to the helplessness of sheep will be either meaningless or even contradictory. Again, a marginal note is in order.

[MAT 7:15](https://ref.ly/Matt7:15) · [MAT 9:36](https://ref.ly/Matt9:36) · [MAT 10:6](https://ref.ly/Matt10:6) · [MAT 10:16](https://ref.ly/Matt10:16) · [MAT 12:11](https://ref.ly/Matt12:11) · [MAT 12:12](https://ref.ly/Matt12:12) · [MAT 15:24](https://ref.ly/Matt15:24) · [MAT 18:12](https://ref.ly/Matt18:12) · [MAT 25:32](https://ref.ly/Matt25:32) · [MAT 25:33](https://ref.ly/Matt25:33) · [MAT 26:31](https://ref.ly/Matt26:31) · [MRK 6:34](https://ref.ly/Matt6:34) · [MRK 14:27](https://ref.ly/Matt14:27) · [LUK 15:4](https://ref.ly/Mark15:4) · [LUK 15:6](https://ref.ly/Mark15:6) · [JHN 2:14](https://ref.ly/Luke2:14) · [JHN 2:15](https://ref.ly/Luke2:15) · [JHN 10:1](https://ref.ly/Luke10:1) · [JHN 10:2](https://ref.ly/Luke10:2) · [JHN 10:3](https://ref.ly/Luke10:3) · [JHN 10:3](https://ref.ly/Luke10:3) · [JHN 10:4](https://ref.ly/Luke10:4) · [ACT 8:32](https://ref.ly/John8:32) · [ROM 8:36](https://ref.ly/Acts8:36) · [HEB 13:20](https://ref.ly/Phlm13:20) · [1PE 2:25](https://ref.ly/Jas2:25) · [REV 18:13](https://ref.ly/Jude18:13)

11\.30 · Groups and Classes of Persons and Members of Such Groups and Classes · Socio\-Religious

a person who is like a sheep (a figurative extension of meaning of πρόβατον\[a] ‘sheep,’ [4\.22](#4.22)) a follower of Christ, with the implication of needing care and guidance Compare [11\.29](#11.29) and [11\.31](#11.31).

[JHN 10:7](https://ref.ly/Luke10:7) · [JHN 10:8](https://ref.ly/Luke10:8) · [JHN 10:11](https://ref.ly/Luke10:11) · [JHN 10:12](https://ref.ly/Luke10:12) · [JHN 10:12](https://ref.ly/Luke10:12) · [JHN 10:13](https://ref.ly/Luke10:13) · [JHN 10:15](https://ref.ly/Luke10:15) · [JHN 10:16](https://ref.ly/Luke10:16) · [JHN 10:26](https://ref.ly/Luke10:26) · [JHN 10:27](https://ref.ly/Luke10:27) · [JHN 21:16](https://ref.ly/Luke21:16) · [JHN 21:17](https://ref.ly/Luke21:17)

1 Since the sheep were approximately the same size as forest deer and since they had a fleece closely resembling wild cotton, they could quite logically be called cotton deer.

## πρόδρομος (id: 004174000000000)

πρόδρομος
=========

πρόδρομος (G4274)

adj \| \-ον \| τρέχω

36\.9 · Guide, Discipline, Follow · Guide, Lead

forerunner (a figurative extension of meaning of πρόδρομος ‘one who runs on ahead,’ not occurring in the NT) one who undergoes an experience in advance of others In rendering πρόδρομος in [HEB 6:20](https://ref.ly/Phlm6:20) it is important to avoid an expression which would suggest that Jesus simply ‘ran on ahead.’ The implication of πρόδρομος in this type of context is that of a precursor, that is to say, one who goes on ahead in order to show the way or to pioneer on behalf of someone else. [HEB 6:20](https://ref.ly/Phlm6:20) may therefore be rendered as ‘Jesus went on ahead of us in there for our benefit.’

[HEB 6:20](https://ref.ly/Phlm6:20)

* **Associated Passages:** Hebrews 6:20

προέχω
======

ἔχω

65\.47 · (προέχομαι) · Value · Advantageous, Not Advantageous

to have a greater benefit, to experience a superior advantage to experience superior benefit or advantage, implying some type of comparison In indicating ‘a superior advantage’ it may be necessary to be quite specific about the basis for the comparison. For example, in [1CO 8:8](https://ref.ly/Rom8:8) it may be necessary to translate as ‘if we do eat, we will be no better off than if we did not eat,’ and in [ROM 3:9](https://ref.ly/Acts3:9) one may translate ‘what then; are we decidedly better off than the others are?’

[ROM 3:9](https://ref.ly/Acts3:9)

## πρόϊμος (id: 004188000000000)

πρόϊμος
=======

πρόϊμος (G4406a)

adj \| \-ον \| ὕω (rain)

14\.14 · Physical Events and States · Rain

early rain, autumn rain a rain occurring early in the autumn, September or October, in contrast with normal rains occurring in the middle of the winter season See [14\.13](#14.13).  
In Palestine the rainy season differs appreciably from what it is in many other parts of the world. In tropical regions north of the Equator, the rains occur primarily from May through October, and in the tropical regions below the Equator, the rains normally occur from November through April. The other seasons of the year are normally quite dry. In Palestine, however, rains occur primarily during the months of November through March, and the summer months are often without rain. Because of this significant difference in the time of rainfall, it may be important to relate the early and late rains not to particular times of the year but to the normal period for rain. For example, the early rains could be ‘rain coming before it is normally expected’ or ‘rain at the beginning of the rainy season,’ and late rains are ‘rains which come later than what one expects’ or ‘rain at the end of the rainy season.’ On the other hand, the contrast may be expressed as ‘rain coming before it should’ or ‘rain coming later than it should.’ It is difficult to relate early and late rains to so\-called ‘growing seasons,’ since in the Middle East grains grow primarily during the rainy season and are harvested early in the spring, while fruits and vegetables are grown in the spring and summer with extensive use made of irrigation.

[JAS 5:7](https://ref.ly/Heb5:7)

* **Associated Passages:** James 5:7


## προΐστημι (id: 004189000000000)

προΐστημι
=========

προΐστημι (G4291) — προΐσταμαι

ἵστημι

36\.1 · (προΐσταμαι\[a]) · Guide, Discipline, Follow · Guide, Lead

to guide, to direct, to lead to so influence others as to cause them to follow a recommended course of action In some languages it is difficult to distinguish readily between expressions for ‘leading’ and those which refer to ‘ruling’ or ‘governing,’ but it is important to try to distinguish clearly between these two different sets of interpersonal relations. In some languages, the concept of ‘leading’ can be expressed by ‘showing how to’ or ‘demonstrating how one ought to.’ In other languages it is possible to speak of ‘leading’ as simply ‘going ahead of,’ but too often such an expression may designate only ‘a scout’ who goes ahead to see whether things are safe, or it may refer only to a person who insists on his prerogative as the most distinguished person in a group.  
The phrase ‘in the Lord’ in [1TH 5:12](https://ref.ly/Col5:12) probably refers to matters concerning the Christian life.

[ROM 12:8](https://ref.ly/Acts12:8) · [1TH 5:12](https://ref.ly/Col5:12) · [1TI 3:4](https://ref.ly/2Thess3:4) · [1TI 3:5](https://ref.ly/2Thess3:5) · [1TI 3:12](https://ref.ly/2Thess3:12)

35\.12 · (προΐσταμαι\[b]) · Help, Care For · Help

to be active in helping, to be involved in giving aid to be engaged in helping or aiding

[ROM 12:8](https://ref.ly/Acts12:8) · [TIT 3:8](https://ref.ly/2Tim3:8)

68\.67 · (προΐσταμαι\[c]) · Aspect · Do Intensely or Extensively

to strive for, to devote oneself to to engage in something with intense devotion

[1TI 5:17](https://ref.ly/2Thess5:17) · [TIT 3:14](https://ref.ly/2Tim3:14)1

1 TIT 3:14 It is possible to analyze the use of προΐστημι in [TIT 3:14](https://ref.ly/2Tim3:14) as being a matter of case relations (Domain 90\).

* **Associated Passages:** Romans 12:8; 1 Thessalonians 5:12; 1 Timothy 3:4; 1 Timothy 3:5; 1 Timothy 3:12; Titus 3:8; 1 Timothy 5:17; Titus 3:14

In [GAL 3:8](https://ref.ly/2Cor3:8) προοράω is used with the Scripture as the subject, in which case it would be the personified Scripture as knowing beforehand, but it is also possible to understand προοράω in [GAL 3:8](https://ref.ly/2Cor3:8) as meaning to cause something to be known ahead of time.

15\.72 · Linear Movement · Leave, Depart, Flee, Escape, Send

to send on one’s way, to help on one’s way to send someone on in the direction in which he has already been moving, with the probable implication of providing help

[ACT 15:3](https://ref.ly/John15:3) · [ROM 15:24](https://ref.ly/Acts15:24) · [1CO 16:6](https://ref.ly/Rom16:6) · [1CO 16:11](https://ref.ly/Rom16:11) · [2CO 1:16](https://ref.ly/1Cor1:16) · [TIT 3:13](https://ref.ly/2Tim3:13) · [3JN 1:6](https://ref.ly/2John1:6)

15\.155 · Linear Movement · Come/Go With, Travel With

to escort, to accompany to accompany a person for a short distance at the beginning of a journey

[ACT 20:38](https://ref.ly/John20:38) · [ACT 21:5](https://ref.ly/John21:5)

* **Associated Passages:** Acts 15:3; Romans 15:24; 1 Corinthians 16:6; 1 Corinthians 16:11; 2 Corinthians 1:16; Titus 3:13; 3 John 1:6; Acts 20:38; Acts 21:5


## προπετής (id: 004212000000000)

προπετής
========

προπετής (G4312)

adj \| \-ές, \-οῦς \| πίπτω

88\.98 · Moral and Ethical Qualities and Related Behavior · Sensible Behavior, Senseless Behavior

reckless, impetuous pertaining to impetuous and reckless behavior In some instances it may be possible to translate προπετής in [ACT 19:36](https://ref.ly/John19:36) as ‘without thinking’ or ‘without counting the cost.’

[ACT 19:36](https://ref.ly/John19:36) · [2TI 3:4](https://ref.ly/1Tim3:4)

* **Associated Passages:** Acts 19:36; 2 Timothy 3:4


## προπορεύομαι (id: 004213000000000)

προπορεύομαι
============

προπορεύομαι (G4313)

πορεύομαι

15\.143 · Linear Movement · Come/Go In Front Of

to go in front of, to precede to move in front of or ahead of, with the implication that both parties are moving in the same direction

[LUK 1:76](https://ref.ly/Mark1:76)1

15\.181 · Linear Movement · Lead, Bring, Take

to lead, to show the way to to go in front of, in order to show the way

[ACT 7:40](https://ref.ly/John7:40)

1 LUK 1:76 It is possible that προπορεύομαι in [LUK 1:76](https://ref.ly/Mark1:76) should be interpreted as temporal rather than as spacial.

* **Associated Passages:** Luke 1:76; Acts 7:40

90\.25 · Case · Content

about, to (with the accusative) a marker of content, particularly when persons are involved and/or the context suggests some type of response being made For a different interpretation of πρός in [MRK 12:12](https://ref.ly/Matt12:12) and [COL 3:13](https://ref.ly/Phil3:13), see [90\.33](#90.33).

89\.112 · Relations · Association

with, before a marker of association, often with the implication of interrelationships

for the purpose of, for the sake of, in order to a marker of purpose, pointing to the goal of an event or state

89\.44 · Relations · Result

result in, end in, have as a consequence a marker of result, with focus upon the end point

[JHN 11:4](https://ref.ly/Luke11:4) · [1TI 4:8](https://ref.ly/2Thess4:8) · [1TI 4:8](https://ref.ly/2Thess4:8) · [2TI 3:17](https://ref.ly/1Tim3:17) · [2PE 3:16](https://ref.ly/1Pet3:16) · [1JN 5:16](https://ref.ly/2Pet5:16) · [1JN 5:16](https://ref.ly/2Pet5:16) · [1JN 5:16](https://ref.ly/2Pet5:16) · [1JN 5:17](https://ref.ly/2Pet5:17)

89\.9 · Relations · Relations Involving Correspondences \[Isomorphisms]

in accordance with, according to, in line with a marker of a relation involving correspondence, with the probable implication of some element of reciprocity

[LUK 12:47](https://ref.ly/Mark12:47) · [2CO 5:10](https://ref.ly/1Cor5:10) · [GAL 2:14](https://ref.ly/2Cor2:14)

67\.162 · Time · Indefinite Units of Time: Age, Lifetime, Interval, Period

to, toward a marker of a period of time occurring before a subsequent period, and implying anticipation

[LUK 24:29](https://ref.ly/Mark24:29)

67\.16 · Time · A Point of Time without Reference to Other Points of Time: Time, Occasion, Ever, Often

at a marker of a point of time, probably implying proximity

[HEB 12:11](https://ref.ly/Phlm12:11)

78\.51 · Degree · Up To, As Much As, To the Degree That \[Marked Extent of Degree]

to the point of, to the extent of, to the degree that, up to a degree extending to a particular point as marked by the context

[JHN 4:35](https://ref.ly/Luke4:35) · [JAS 4:5](https://ref.ly/Heb4:5)

89\.7 · Relations · Specification

with regard to, with, between ... and a marker of a relation involving potential interaction

90\.20 · Case · Viewpoint Participant

in the sight of, in the opinion of, in the judgment of (with the accusative) marking a participant whose viewpoint is relevant to an event3

[JHN 5:45](https://ref.ly/Luke5:45) · [ACT 2:47](https://ref.ly/John2:47) · [ACT 24:16](https://ref.ly/John24:16) · [ROM 4:2](https://ref.ly/Acts4:2) · [2CO 12:21](https://ref.ly/1Cor12:21) · [1TH 4:12](https://ref.ly/Col4:12)

90\.33 · Case · Opposition

against (with the accusative) a marker of opposition, with the probable implication of a reaction or response to a previous event For another interpretation of πρός in [MRK 12:12](https://ref.ly/Matt12:12) and [COL 3:13](https://ref.ly/Phil3:13), see [90\.25](#90.25).

64\.17 · Comparison

with, in comparison with, to be compared to a marker of that which is compared to something else

[ROM 8:18](https://ref.ly/Acts8:18)

67\.109 · Time · Duration of Time without Reference to Points or Units of Time: Time, Spend Time, Always, Eternal, Old, Immediately, Young

**Collocation:** πρὸς καιρόν · πρὸς ὥραν

not long, temporary, for a little while, for a while pertaining to a relatively short period of time, with emphasis upon the temporary nature of the event or state

[LUK 8:13](https://ref.ly/Mark8:13) · [JHN 5:35](https://ref.ly/Luke5:35) · [1CO 7:5](https://ref.ly/Rom7:5) · [2CO 7:8](https://ref.ly/1Cor7:8) · [GAL 2:5](https://ref.ly/2Cor2:5) · [1TH 2:17](https://ref.ly/Col2:17) · [PHM 1:15](https://ref.ly/Titus1:15)

39\.19 · Hostility, Strife · Resistance

**Collocation:** πρὸς κέντρα λακτίζω

to hurt oneself by reacting against a person or command (an idiom, literally: to kick against the goad) to react against authority in such a way as to cause harm or suffering to oneself

[ACT 26:14](https://ref.ly/John26:14)1

67\.106 · Time · Duration of Time without Reference to Points or Units of Time: Time, Spend Time, Always, Eternal, Old, Immediately, Young

**Collocation:** πρὸς ὀλίγον

a little while, for a little while, a short time, brief, briefly pertaining to a relatively brief extent of time4

[HEB 12:10](https://ref.ly/Phlm12:10) · [JAS 4:14](https://ref.ly/Heb4:14)

28\.73 · Know · Not Able To Be Known, Secret

**Collocation:** πρὸς τὸ οὖς

in secret, in private, privately (idiom, literally: to the ear) pertaining to what is known only by those who hear a particular message in private A phrase such as ‘whatever you hear in secret’ may be rendered as ‘whatever you hear when no one else is listening.’  
For another treatment of the phrase πρὸς τὸ οὖς λαλέω, see [33\.91](#33.91).

[LUK 12:3](https://ref.ly/Mark12:3)

33\.91 · Communication · Speak, Talk

**Collocation:** πρὸς τὸ οὖς λαλέω

to whisper, to speak quietly (an idiom, literally: to speak to the ear) to speak quietly and presumably to a restricted audience For another treatment of the phrase πρὸς τὸ οὖς, see [28\.73](#28.73).

[LUK 12:3](https://ref.ly/Mark12:3)

1 ACT 26:14 The expression πρὸς κέντρα λακτίζειν ‘to kick against the goad’ is an idiom (see [39\.19](#39.19)), but within the idiom πρός is to be understood in the sense of ‘against.’ The meaning of the idiom is to oppose, in one way or another, a dominant force. 2 There are no doubt certain subtle distinctions in the meanings of the terms in [83\.9](#83.9) (μέσος\[a], ἀνὰ μέσον, ἐντός\[a], ἐν\[b], μετά\[h], εἰς\[e], ἐπί\[c], πρός\[d], παρά\[b]). For example, πρός\[d] may suggest some kind of interaction or response and παρά\[b] may focus upon association, but it is difficult, if not impossible, on the basis of existing contexts to define precisely the differences in meaning. 3 There are undoubtedly certain subtle distinctions of meaning associated with the various markers in [90\.20](#90.20), but it is impossible to determine those differences on the basis of existing contexts. 4 The series of expressions indicating relative shortness of time may very well differ slightly, at least in connotative meaning, but on the basis of existing NT contexts it is not possible to determine significant denotative differences. The phrases in this series (ἐν ὀλίγῳ, δι᾽ ὀλίγων, πρὸς ὀλίγον, διὰ βραχέων, μικρός\[d]) may be technically regarded as idioms, but functionally they are better classed as frozen phrases, which act essentially like morphological units.

## προσάββατον (id: 004215000000000)

προσάββατον
===========

προσάββατον (G4315)

noun \| \-ου \| σάββατον

67\.202 · Time · Units of Time with Reference to Other Units or Points of Time: Yesterday, Today, Next Day

the day before the Sabbath, Friday the day immediately before the Sabbath

[MRK 15:42](https://ref.ly/Matt15:42)

* **Associated Passages:** Mark 15:42


## προσαγορεύω (id: 004216000000000)

προσαγορεύω
===========

προσαγορεύω (G4316)

ἀγορεύω (speak)

33\.127 · Communication · Name

to call, to give a name to, to give a title to to give a name or title to

[HEB 5:10](https://ref.ly/Phlm5:10)

* **Associated Passages:** Hebrews 5:10


## προσάγω (id: 004217000000000)

προσάγω
=======

προσάγω (G4317)

aor.inf. προσαγαγειν · aor.pass. προσήχθην \| ἄγω

15\.172 · Linear Movement · Lead, Bring, Take

to lead before, to bring into the presence of, to bring to to bring or lead into the presence of someone

[LUK 9:41](https://ref.ly/Mark9:41) · [ACT 16:20](https://ref.ly/John16:20) · [1PE 3:18](https://ref.ly/Jas3:18)

15\.77 · Linear Movement · Come Near, Approach

to move toward, to approach, to come near to to move toward a reference point, with a possible implication in certain contexts of a reciprocal relationship between the person approaching and the one who is approached

[ACT 27:27](https://ref.ly/John27:27)

* **Associated Passages:** Luke 9:41; Acts 16:20; 1 Peter 3:18; Acts 27:27


## προσαγωγή (id: 004218000000000)

προσαγωγή
=========

προσαγωγή (G4318)

noun \| \-ῆς \| ἄγω

33\.72 · Communication · Speak, Talk

approach, access the right or opportunity to address someone, implying higher status of the person addressed

[ROM 5:2](https://ref.ly/Acts5:2) · [EPH 2:18](https://ref.ly/Gal2:18) · [EPH 3:12](https://ref.ly/Gal3:12)

* **Associated Passages:** Romans 5:2; Ephesians 2:18; Ephesians 3:12


## προσαιτέω (id: 004219000000000)

προσαιτέω
=========

προσαιτέω (G4319)

noun \| αἰτέω

33\.173 · Communication · Ask For, Request

to beg to ask for charity This word is found as a variant reading only: τυφλὸς ἐκάθητο παρὰ τὴν ὁδὸν προσαιτῶν ‘a blind man was sitting and begging by the road’ [MRK 10:46](https://ref.ly/Matt10:46) (apparatus).

[MRK 10:46](https://ref.ly/Matt10:46) · [JHN 9:8](https://ref.ly/Luke9:8)

* **Associated Passages:** Mark 10:46; John 9:8


## προσαίτης (id: 004220000000000)

προσαίτης
=========

προσαίτης (G4319a)

noun \| \-ου \| αἰτέω

33\.174 · Communication · Ask For, Request

beggar (derivative of προσαιτέω ‘to beg,’ [33\.173](#33.173)) one who asks for charity

[MRK 10:46](https://ref.ly/Matt10:46) · [JHN 9:8](https://ref.ly/Luke9:8)

* **Associated Passages:** Mark 10:46; John 9:8


## προσαναβαίνω (id: 004221000000000)

προσαναβαίνω
============

προσαναβαίνω (G4320)

aor.impv. προσανάβηθι \| βαίνω (go, walk)

15\.106 · Linear Movement · Come/Go Up, Ascend

to move up to, to come up to, to go up to to move up to a point (with the possible implication of higher status) It may be necessary in some languages to translate this expression in [LUK 14:10](https://ref.ly/Mark14:10) as ‘come up to a higher place’ or ‘come up to a better seat’ or ‘come up to a more important place.’

[LUK 14:10](https://ref.ly/Mark14:10)

* **Associated Passages:** Luke 14:10


## προσαναλίσκω (id: 004222000000000)

προσαναλίσκω
============

προσαναλίσκω (G4321)

ἁλῶναι \[ἁλίσκομαι] (be caught, captured)

57\.148 · Possess, Transfer, Exchange · Spend, Waste

to spend a lot, to spend much to spend excessively

[LUK 8:43](https://ref.ly/Mark8:43)

* **Associated Passages:** Luke 8:43


## προσαναπληρόω (id: 004223000000000)

προσαναπληρόω
=============

προσαναπληρόω (G4322)

πληρόω

35\.34 · Help, Care For · Provide For, Support

to provide fully to provide sufficiently in addition (a more emphatic expression than πληρόω\[d], [35\.33](#35.33))

[2CO 9:12](https://ref.ly/1Cor9:12) · [2CO 11:9](https://ref.ly/1Cor11:9)

* **Associated Passages:** 2 Corinthians 9:12; 2 Corinthians 11:9


## προσανατίθημι (id: 004224000000000)

προσανατίθημι
=============

προσανατίθημι (G4323) — προσανατίθεμαι

τίθεμαι

59\.72 · (προσανατίθεμαι\[a]) · Quantity · Add, Subtract

to add to add something to an existing quantity In a number of languages it is not at all difficult to speak of ‘adding things,’ but to add events may provide some special difficulties in restructuring. For example, in [LUK 3:20](https://ref.ly/Mark3:20) it may be important to translate as ‘he had done many other bad things, but he also did this,’ and in [GAL 2:6](https://ref.ly/2Cor2:6) it may be helpful to translate ‘for those who were of repute did not say that I should do anything more than I was doing.’

[GAL 2:6](https://ref.ly/2Cor2:6)

33\.175 · (προσανατίθεμαι\[b]) · Communication · Ask For, Request

to consult with, to ask advice of to ask someone for advice In rendering ‘to ask advice of,’ one may often employ an expression such as ‘to ask what one should do’ or ‘to ask, What shall I do?’

[GAL 1:16](https://ref.ly/2Cor1:16)

* **Associated Passages:** Galatians 2:6; Galatians 1:16


## προσανέχω (id: 004225000000000)

προσανέχω
=========

προσανέχω

ἔχω

15\.77 · Linear Movement · Come Near, Approach

to move toward, to approach, to come near to to move toward a reference point, with a possible implication in certain contexts of a reciprocal relationship between the person approaching and the one who is approached This word is found as a variant reading only: ὑπενόουν οἱ ναῦται προσανέχειν τινὰ αὐτοῖς χώραν ‘the sailors sensed that they were approaching some land’ [ACT 27:27](https://ref.ly/John27:27) (apparatus).

[ACT 27:27](https://ref.ly/John27:27)

* **Associated Passages:** Acts 27:27


## προσαπειλέω (id: 004226000000000)

προσαπειλέω
===========

προσαπειλέω (G4324) — προσαπειλέομαι

ἀπειλέω

33\.292 · (προσαπειλέομαι) · Communication · Threaten

to threaten further to add to or to extend one’s threats It is also possible to understand προσαπειλέομαι in [ACT 4:21](https://ref.ly/John4:21) as meaning ‘to threaten besides.’

[ACT 4:21](https://ref.ly/John4:21)

* **Associated Passages:** Acts 4:21


## προσαχέω (id: 004227000000000)

προσαχέω
========

προσαχέω

ἠχέω

14\.81 · Physical Events and States · Sound

to resound (an alternative form of προσηχέω, not occurring in the NT) to reflect back a sound προσαχέω occurs only in the first hand of the Vaticanus manuscript of [ACT 27:27](https://ref.ly/John27:27), where the best textual authorities read προσάγειν in the phrase ὑπενόουν οἱ ναῦται προσάγειν τινὰ αὐτοῖς χώραν ‘the sailors suspected that they were getting close to land.’ With the verb προσαχεῖν the meaning would be ‘the sailors suspected that there was an echoing sound coming from the land.’

[ACT 27:27](https://ref.ly/John27:27)

* **Associated Passages:** Acts 27:27


## προσβιβάζω (id: 004228000000000)

προσβιβάζω
==========

προσβιβάζω

33\.299 · Communication · Urge, Persuade

to urge to speak in such a way as to encourage a particular type of behavior or action This word is found as a variant reading only: ἐκ δὲ τοῦ ὄχλου προσεβίβασαν Ἀλέξανδρον ‘some from the crowd urged Alexander’ [ACT 19:33](https://ref.ly/John19:33) (apparatus).

[ACT 19:33](https://ref.ly/John19:33)

* **Associated Passages:** Acts 19:33


## προσδαπανάω (id: 004229000000000)

προσδαπανάω
===========

προσδαπανάω (G4325)

δαπανάω

57\.147 · Possess, Transfer, Exchange · Spend, Waste

to spend in addition to pay out or spend in addition

[LUK 10:35](https://ref.ly/Mark10:35)

* **Associated Passages:** Luke 10:35


## προσδέομαι (id: 004230000000000)

προσδέομαι
==========

προσδέομαι (G4326)

δέομαι

57\.45 · Possess, Transfer, Exchange · Need, Lack

to be in need of something more, to lack something additional to need or lack something further or in addition One may also render [ACT 17:25](https://ref.ly/John17:25) as ‘there is nothing that he needs that people can supply by working for him.’

[ACT 17:25](https://ref.ly/John17:25)

* **Associated Passages:** Acts 17:25


## προσδέχομαι (id: 004231000000000)

προσδέχομαι
===========

προσδέχομαι (G4327)

δέχομαι

31\.53 · Hold a View, Believe, Trust · Accept As True

to accept, to receive, to hold to accept a message for oneself and to act accordingly

[ACT 24:15](https://ref.ly/John24:15) · [HEB 10:34](https://ref.ly/Phlm10:34) · [HEB 11:35](https://ref.ly/Phlm11:35)

34\.53 · Association · Welcome, Receive

to welcome, to receive, to accept, to have as a guest to accept the presence of a person with friendliness1

[LUK 15:2](https://ref.ly/Mark15:2) · [ROM 16:2](https://ref.ly/Acts16:2) · [PHP 2:29](https://ref.ly/Eph2:29) · [JUD 1:21](https://ref.ly/3John1:21)

85\.60 · Existence in Space · Remain, Stay

to await, to wait for to remain in a place and/or state, with expectancy concerning a future event2

[MRK 15:43](https://ref.ly/Matt15:43) · [LUK 2:25](https://ref.ly/Mark2:25) · [LUK 2:38](https://ref.ly/Mark2:38) · [LUK 12:36](https://ref.ly/Mark12:36) · [LUK 23:51](https://ref.ly/Mark23:51) · [ACT 23:21](https://ref.ly/John23:21) · [TIT 2:13](https://ref.ly/2Tim2:13)

1 There are no doubt certain contextual constraints involved in the choice of ἀναδέχομαι\[a], ἀποδέχομαι\[a], ἐπιδέχομαι\[a], εἰσδέχομαι, ὑποδέχομαι, προσδέχομαι\[b], and παραδέχομαι\[b] in the sense that the spacial relationships involved in the welcoming of a guest can be reflected in the prepositional prefixes. For example, in [LUK 8:40](https://ref.ly/Mark8:40) the use of ἀποδέχομαι may focus upon the particular source, while ἐπιδέχομαι in [3JN 1:10](https://ref.ly/2John1:10) may be relevant in view of the fact that the believers are coming to a particular point. προσδέχομαι in [LUK 15:2](https://ref.ly/Mark15:2) may suggest a kind of reciprocal relationship which is established with outcasts, and παραδέχομαι in [ACT 15:4](https://ref.ly/John15:4) may simply point to spacial proximity. However, it would be wrong to insist upon clear distinctions in meaning based on the very limited number of contexts involved. Some of these same distinctions in spacial relations may exist for the set προσλαμβάνω\[e], παραλαμβάνω\[d], and ἀπολαμβάνω\[d]. 2 There are probably certain subtle distinctions of meaning in this set. The terms containing the stem μεν\- probably focus more upon location, while with the stem δεχ\- the focus may be more upon anticipation, but there is no consistent patterning on the basis of this type of distinction.

* **Associated Passages:** Acts 24:15; Hebrews 10:34; Hebrews 11:35; Luke 15:2; Romans 16:2; Philippians 2:29; Jude 1:21; Mark 15:43; Luke 2:25; Luke 2:38; Luke 12:36; Luke 23:51; Acts 23:21; Titus 2:13


## προσδοκάω (id: 004232000000000)

προσδοκάω
=========

προσδοκάω (G4328)

δοκέω

25\.228 · Attitudes and Emotions · Worry, Anxiety, Distress, Peace

to wait with apprehension, to wait with anxiety to await with apprehension concerning impending danger or trouble

[LUK 1:21](https://ref.ly/Mark1:21) · [ACT 27:33](https://ref.ly/John27:33)1

30\.55 · Think · To Think Concerning Future Contingencies

to expect, to anticipate, expectation to expect something to happen, whether good or bad

[MAT 11:3](https://ref.ly/Matt11:3) · [MAT 24:50](https://ref.ly/Matt24:50) · [LUK 3:15](https://ref.ly/Mark3:15) · [LUK 7:19](https://ref.ly/Mark7:19) · [LUK 7:20](https://ref.ly/Mark7:20) · [LUK 8:40](https://ref.ly/Mark8:40) · [LUK 12:46](https://ref.ly/Mark12:46) · [ACT 3:5](https://ref.ly/John3:5) · [ACT 10:24](https://ref.ly/John10:24) · [ACT 28:6](https://ref.ly/John28:6) · [ACT 28:6](https://ref.ly/John28:6) · [2PE 3:12](https://ref.ly/1Pet3:12) · [2PE 3:13](https://ref.ly/1Pet3:13) · [2PE 3:14](https://ref.ly/1Pet3:14)

1 ACT 27:33 In [ACT 27:33](https://ref.ly/John27:33) προσδοκάω includes two significant semantic features: (1\) waiting over a period of time and (2\) apprehension and worry with regard to the outcome of the severe storm. It would be possible to classify προσδοκάω as simply continuing in a state over a period of time, but particularly in the context of [ACT 27:33](https://ref.ly/John27:33) the element of apprehension and worry seems to be dominant.

* **Associated Passages:** Luke 1:21; Acts 27:33; Matthew 11:3; Matthew 24:50; Luke 3:15; Luke 7:19; Luke 7:20; Luke 8:40; Luke 12:46; Acts 3:5; Acts 10:24; Acts 28:6; 2 Peter 3:12; 2 Peter 3:13; 2 Peter 3:14

## προσεγγίζω (id: 004235000000000)

προσεγγίζω
==========

προσεγγίζω

ἐγγίζω

15\.77 · Linear Movement · Come Near, Approach

to move toward, to approach, to come near to to move toward a reference point, with a possible implication in certain contexts of a reciprocal relationship between the person approaching and the one who is approached This word is found as a variant reading only: κατὰ μέσον τῆς νυκτὸς ὑπενόουν οἱ ναῦται προσεγγίζειν τινὰ αὐτοῖς χώραν ‘in the middle of the night the sailors sensed that they were approaching some land’ [ACT 27:27](https://ref.ly/John27:27) (apparatus).

[ACT 27:27](https://ref.ly/John27:27)

## προσεργάζομαι (id: 004236000000000)

προσεργάζομαι
=============

προσεργάζομαι (G4333)

ἐργάζομαι

57\.191 · Possess, Transfer, Exchange · Earn, Gain, Do Business

to make more, to earn in addition to earn or to gain something in addition As in the case of [LUK 19:18](https://ref.ly/Mark19:18) (see [57\.189](#57.189)), it may be necessary to change the form of expression so as to introduce a personal agent, for example, ‘I have earned ten minas with the one you gave me.’ In some languages it may be necessary to say ‘I did business with the one mina and now I have ten’ or ‘... it has become ten minas.’

[LUK 19:16](https://ref.ly/Mark19:16)

34\.23 · Association · Join, Begin To Associate

to undertake to join with, to seek association with to take the initiative in association with someone Though it is also true that Jews were not allowed to continue to associate with Gentiles, the focus of προσέρχομαι\[b] in [ACT 10:28](https://ref.ly/John10:28) appears to be upon the initiative which could not be taken by a Jew in approaching a Gentile in order to be associated in some activity which would be contrary to ritual observances. προσέρχομαι in this context may therefore be rendered in some languages as 'to join arms with a Gentile, ́ 'to link oneself with a Gentile, ́ or 'to become a close friend of a Gentile. ́

[ACT 10:28](https://ref.ly/John10:28) · [HEB 11:6](https://ref.ly/Phlm11:6)

31\.19 · Hold a View, Believe, Trust · Agree, Consent

to come to an opinion with, to agree with (a figurative extension of meaning of προσέρχομαι\[a] ‘to move toward,’ [15\.77](#15.77)) to come to a position of holding the same opinion as someone else

[1TI 6:3](https://ref.ly/2Thess6:3)

## προσευχή (id: 004238000000000)

προσευχή
========

προσευχή (G4335)

noun \| \-ῆς \| εὔχομαι

33\.178 · Communication · Pray

prayer the act of speaking to or making requests of God1 In some languages there are a number of different terms used for prayer depending upon the nature of the content, for example, requests for material blessing, pleas for spiritual help, intercession for others, thanksgiving, and praise. There may also be important distinctions on the basis of urgency and need. The most generic expression for prayer may simply be ‘to speak to God.’ It is normally best to avoid an expression which means primarily ‘to recite.’

a place for prayer (derivative of προσεύχομαι ‘to pray,’ [33\.178](#33.178)) a place where people customarily meet to pray

adj \| \-ον \| καιρός

not long, temporary, for a little while, for a while pertaining to a relatively short period of time, with emphasis upon the temporary nature of the event or state

## προσκαλέω (id: 004244000000000)

προσκαλέω
=========

προσκαλέω (G4341) — προσκαλέομαι

καλέω

33\.308 · (προσκαλέομαι\[a]) · Communication · Call

to call, to call to to call to, with a possible implication of a reciprocal relation

[MAT 15:10](https://ref.ly/Matt15:10) · [MAT 15:32](https://ref.ly/Matt15:32) · [MAT 18:2](https://ref.ly/Matt18:2) · [MAT 18:32](https://ref.ly/Matt18:32) · [MAT 20:25](https://ref.ly/Matt20:25) · [MRK 3:13](https://ref.ly/Matt3:13) · [MRK 3:23](https://ref.ly/Matt3:23) · [MRK 6:7](https://ref.ly/Matt6:7) · [MRK 7:14](https://ref.ly/Matt7:14) · [MRK 8:1](https://ref.ly/Matt8:1) · [MRK 8:34](https://ref.ly/Matt8:34) · [MRK 10:42](https://ref.ly/Matt10:42) · [MRK 12:43](https://ref.ly/Matt12:43) · [MRK 15:44](https://ref.ly/Matt15:44) · [LUK 7:18](https://ref.ly/Mark7:18) · [LUK 15:26](https://ref.ly/Mark15:26) · [LUK 16:5](https://ref.ly/Mark16:5) · [LUK 18:16](https://ref.ly/Mark18:16) · [ACT 2:39](https://ref.ly/John2:39) · [ACT 5:40](https://ref.ly/John5:40) · [ACT 6:2](https://ref.ly/John6:2) · [ACT 13:7](https://ref.ly/John13:7) · [ACT 23:17](https://ref.ly/John23:17) · [ACT 23:18](https://ref.ly/John23:18) · [ACT 23:23](https://ref.ly/John23:23)

33\.312 · (προσκαλέομαι\[b]) · Communication · Call

to call, to call to a task to urgently invite someone to accept responsibilities for a particular task, implying a new relationship to the one who does the calling1 It is rare that one can translate καλέω\[d], κλῆσις\[a], and προσκαλέω\[b] as simply ‘to call’ in the sense of ‘to speak to someone at a distance and tell them to come.’ For example, in [2TH 2:14](https://ref.ly/1Thess2:14) it may be necessary to render the above clause as ‘through the good news we preached, which summoned you to do this.’ Similarly, in [ACT 16:10](https://ref.ly/John16:10) one may translate ‘because God has urgently invited us to preach the good news to the people there.’

[MAT 10:1](https://ref.ly/Matt10:1) · [ACT 13:2](https://ref.ly/John13:2) · [ACT 16:10](https://ref.ly/John16:10) · [JAS 5:14](https://ref.ly/Heb5:14)

1 It is possible that προσκαλέομαι\[b] differs in meaning from καλέω\[d] in emphasizing more specifically either the person who is called or the task to which one is called.

* **Associated Passages:** Matthew 15:10; Matthew 15:32; Matthew 18:2; Matthew 18:32; Matthew 20:25; Mark 3:13; Mark 3:23; Mark 6:7; Mark 7:14; Mark 8:1; Mark 8:34; Mark 10:42; Mark 12:43; Mark 15:44; Luke 7:18; Luke 15:26; Luke 16:5; Luke 18:16; Acts 2:39; Acts 5:40; Acts 6:2; Acts 13:7; Acts 23:17; Acts 23:18; Acts 23:23; Matthew 10:1; Acts 13:2; Acts 16:10; James 5:14

προσκαρτερέω
============

προσκαρτερέω (G4342)

καρτερέω

68\.68 · Aspect · Do Intensely or Extensively

to devote oneself to, to keep on, to persist in to continue to do something with intense effort, with the possible implication of despite difficulty

[ACT 1:14](https://ref.ly/John1:14) · [ACT 2:42](https://ref.ly/John2:42) · [ACT 2:46](https://ref.ly/John2:46) · [ACT 6:4](https://ref.ly/John6:4) · [ROM 12:12](https://ref.ly/Acts12:12) · [ROM 13:6](https://ref.ly/Acts13:6) · [COL 4:2](https://ref.ly/Phil4:2)

34\.2 · Association · Associate

to stay close to, to associate closely with to associate closely and continuously with

[ACT 8:13](https://ref.ly/John8:13)

35\.28 · Help, Care For · Serve

to serve personally to serve in a close personal relationship The phrase ‘his personal servants’ can be rendered as ‘those who serve just him.’  
In [MRK 3:9](https://ref.ly/Matt3:9) a literal translation of the phrase ἵνα πλοιάριον προσκαρτερῇ αὐτῷ as ‘that a boat may be in personal attendance to him,’ may be rather awkward in some languages. A more natural rendering would be ‘... may wait upon him’ or ‘... may be ready at hand for him to use.’

[MRK 3:9](https://ref.ly/Matt3:9) · [ACT 10:7](https://ref.ly/John10:7)

προσκυνέω
=========

προσκυνέω (G4352)

53\.56 · Religious Activities · Worship, Reverence

to prostrate oneself in worship, to bow down and worship, to worship to express by attitude and possibly by position one’s allegiance to and regard for deity1

[MAT 2:2](https://ref.ly/Matt2:2) · [MAT 2:8](https://ref.ly/Matt2:8) · [MAT 2:11](https://ref.ly/Matt2:11) · [MAT 4:9](https://ref.ly/Matt4:9) · [MAT 4:10](https://ref.ly/Matt4:10) · [MAT 14:33](https://ref.ly/Matt14:33) · [MAT 28:9](https://ref.ly/Matt28:9) · [MAT 28:17](https://ref.ly/Matt28:17) · [LUK 4:7](https://ref.ly/Mark4:7) · [LUK 4:8](https://ref.ly/Mark4:8) · [LUK 24:52](https://ref.ly/Mark24:52) · [JHN 4:20](https://ref.ly/Luke4:20) · [JHN 4:20](https://ref.ly/Luke4:20) · [JHN 4:21](https://ref.ly/Luke4:21) · [JHN 4:22](https://ref.ly/Luke4:22) · [JHN 4:22](https://ref.ly/Luke4:22) · [JHN 4:23](https://ref.ly/Luke4:23) · [JHN 4:23](https://ref.ly/Luke4:23) · [JHN 4:24](https://ref.ly/Luke4:24) · [JHN 4:24](https://ref.ly/Luke4:24) · [JHN 12:20](https://ref.ly/Luke12:20) · [ACT 7:43](https://ref.ly/John7:43) · [ACT 8:27](https://ref.ly/John8:27) · [ACT 24:11](https://ref.ly/John24:11) · [1CO 14:25](https://ref.ly/Rom14:25) · [HEB 1:6](https://ref.ly/Phlm1:6) · [HEB 11:21](https://ref.ly/Phlm11:21) · [REV 4:10](https://ref.ly/Jude4:10) · [REV 5:14](https://ref.ly/Jude5:14) · [REV 7:11](https://ref.ly/Jude7:11) · [REV 9:20](https://ref.ly/Jude9:20) · [REV 11:1](https://ref.ly/Jude11:1) · [REV 11:16](https://ref.ly/Jude11:16) · [REV 13:4](https://ref.ly/Jude13:4) · [REV 13:4](https://ref.ly/Jude13:4) · [REV 13:8](https://ref.ly/Jude13:8) · [REV 13:12](https://ref.ly/Jude13:12) · [REV 13:15](https://ref.ly/Jude13:15) · [REV 14:7](https://ref.ly/Jude14:7) · [REV 14:9](https://ref.ly/Jude14:9) · [REV 14:11](https://ref.ly/Jude14:11) · [REV 15:4](https://ref.ly/Jude15:4) · [REV 16:2](https://ref.ly/Jude16:2) · [REV 19:4](https://ref.ly/Jude19:4) · [REV 19:10](https://ref.ly/Jude19:10) · [REV 19:10](https://ref.ly/Jude19:10) · [REV 19:20](https://ref.ly/Jude19:20) · [REV 20:4](https://ref.ly/Jude20:4) · [REV 22:8](https://ref.ly/Jude22:8) · [REV 22:9](https://ref.ly/Jude22:9)

17\.21 · Stances and Events Related to Stances · Prostrate as an Act of Reverence or Supplication

to prostrate oneself before to prostrate oneself before someone as an act of reverence, fear, or supplication2 προσκυνέω\[b] is semantically very complex in that it indicates not only a body position but also an attitude and activity of reverence or honor. In many contexts it is not necessary to specify both semantic elements, but one or the other may be selected depending upon what seems to be the focus of attention. For example, in [MRK 15:19](https://ref.ly/Matt15:19) one may either translate ‘they knelt before him and worshiped him in a mocking way’ or ‘they knelt before him and prostrated themselves before him.’

[MAT 8:2](https://ref.ly/Matt8:2) · [MAT 9:18](https://ref.ly/Matt9:18) · [MAT 15:25](https://ref.ly/Matt15:25) · [MAT 18:26](https://ref.ly/Matt18:26) · [MAT 20:20](https://ref.ly/Matt20:20) · [MRK 5:6](https://ref.ly/Matt5:6) · [MRK 15:19](https://ref.ly/Matt15:19) · [JHN 9:38](https://ref.ly/Luke9:38) · [ACT 10:25](https://ref.ly/John10:25) · [REV 3:9](https://ref.ly/Jude3:9)

1 προσκυνέω\[a] appears to differ somewhat in meaning from σέβομαισεβάζομαι, and εὐσεβέω\[a] to worship ([53\.53](#53.53)) in emphasizing more the semantic component of position or attitude involved in worship. 2 προσκυνέω may involve the meaning of worship without reference to a prostrate position. For example, in [HEB 11:21](https://ref.ly/Phlm11:21), προσεκύνησεν ἐπὶ τὸ ἄκρον τῆς ῥάβδου αὐτοῦ he leaned on the top of his walking stick and worshiped God, there is evidently no suggestion of a prostrate position, and this particular meaning of προσκυνέω is treated under Religious Activities (see [53\.56](#53.56)).

* **Associated Passages:** Matthew 2:2; Matthew 2:8; Matthew 2:11; Matthew 4:9; Matthew 4:10; Matthew 14:33; Matthew 28:9; Matthew 28:17; Luke 4:7; Luke 4:8; Luke 24:52; John 4:20; John 4:21; John 4:22; John 4:23; John 4:24; John 12:20; Acts 7:43; Acts 8:27; Acts 24:11; 1 Corinthians 14:25; Hebrews 1:6; Hebrews 11:21; Revelation 4:10; Revelation 5:14; Revelation 7:11; Revelation 9:20; Revelation 11:1; Revelation 11:16; Revelation 13:4; Revelation 13:8; Revelation 13:12; Revelation 13:15; Revelation 14:7; Revelation 14:9; Revelation 14:11; Revelation 15:4; Revelation 16:2; Revelation 19:4; Revelation 19:10; Revelation 19:20; Revelation 20:4; Revelation 22:8; Revelation 22:9; Matthew 8:2; Matthew 9:18; Matthew 15:25; Matthew 18:26; Matthew 20:20; Mark 5:6; Mark 15:19; John 9:38; Acts 10:25; Revelation 3:9

## προσλαμβάνω (id: 004259000000000)

προσλαμβάνω
===========

προσλαμβάνω (G4355) — προσλαμβάνομαι

λαμβάνω

18\.2 · (προσλαμβάνομαι\[a]) · Attachment · Grasp, Hold

to take hold of, to grasp to take hold of or grasp, with focus upon the goal of the motion In a number of languages quite different terms must be employed if one is to take someone by the hand in order to lead him or take someone by the hand in order to rescue him. The difference implied in the presumed force or strength required frequently results in quite different words being used.  
The phrase προσλαμβάνομαι τροφῆς ([ACT 27:36](https://ref.ly/John27:36)) may be interpreted as an idiom meaning ‘to eat’ (see footnote at μετέχω [23\.2](#23.2)).

[ACT 27:33](https://ref.ly/John27:33) · [ACT 27:36](https://ref.ly/John27:36)

15\.167 · (προσλαμβάνομαι\[b]) · Linear Movement · Lead, Bring, Take

to bring along, to take along to take or bring along in addition to oneself It is also possible to interpret προσλαμβάνομαι in [ACT 17:5](https://ref.ly/John17:5) as meaning ‘to gather together’ (see [15\.127](#15.127)).

[ACT 17:5](https://ref.ly/John17:5)

15\.180 · (προσλαμβάνομαι\[c]) · Linear Movement · Lead, Bring, Take

to lead aside, to take aside to take or lead off to oneself

[MAT 16:22](https://ref.ly/Matt16:22) · [MRK 8:32](https://ref.ly/Matt8:32) · [ACT 18:26](https://ref.ly/John18:26)

15\.127 · (προσλαμβάνομαι\[d]) · Linear Movement · Gather, Cause To Come Together

to gather together, to form a group gather together to oneself a group of persons It is also possible that προσλαμβάνομαι in [ACT 17:5](https://ref.ly/John17:5) means ‘to take along in addition to oneself’ (see [15\.167](#15.167)).

[ACT 17:5](https://ref.ly/John17:5)

34\.53 · (προσλαμβάνομαι\[e]) · Association · Welcome, Receive

to welcome, to receive, to accept, to have as a guest to accept the presence of a person with friendliness1

[ACT 28:2](https://ref.ly/John28:2) · [ROM 14:1](https://ref.ly/Acts14:1) · [ROM 14:3](https://ref.ly/Acts14:3) · [ROM 15:7](https://ref.ly/Acts15:7) · [ROM 15:7](https://ref.ly/Acts15:7) · [PHM 1:17](https://ref.ly/Titus1:17)

1 There are no doubt certain contextual constraints involved in the choice of ἀναδέχομαι\[a], ἀποδέχομαι\[a], ἐπιδέχομαι\[a], εἰσδέχομαι, ὑποδέχομαι, προσδέχομαι\[b], and παραδέχομαι\[b] in the sense that the spacial relationships involved in the welcoming of a guest can be reflected in the prepositional prefixes. For example, in [LUK 8:40](https://ref.ly/Mark8:40) the use of ἀποδέχομαι may focus upon the particular source, while ἐπιδέχομαι in [3JN 1:10](https://ref.ly/2John1:10) may be relevant in view of the fact that the believers are coming to a particular point. προσδέχομαι in [LUK 15:2](https://ref.ly/Mark15:2) may suggest a kind of reciprocal relationship which is established with outcasts, and παραδέχομαι in [ACT 15:4](https://ref.ly/John15:4) may simply point to spacial proximity. However, it would be wrong to insist upon clear distinctions in meaning based on the very limited number of contexts involved. Some of these same distinctions in spacial relations may exist for the set προσλαμβάνω\[e], παραλαμβάνω\[d], ἀπολαμβάνω\[d].

* **Associated Passages:** Acts 27:33; Acts 27:36; Acts 17:5; Matthew 16:22; Mark 8:32; Acts 18:26; Acts 28:2; Romans 14:1; Romans 14:3; Romans 15:7; Philemon 1:17

34\.34 · Association · Belong To, Be Included in the Membership of, Be Excluded From

acceptance the acceptance of someone into an association In rendering this expression in [ROM 11:15](https://ref.ly/Acts11:15) it may be necessary to indicate clearly the participants involved. For example, one may translate ‘what will happen when God accepts them? It will be the same as having risen from the dead’ or ‘... will be the resurrection of the dead.’

[ROM 11:15](https://ref.ly/Acts11:15)

* **Associated Passages:** Romans 11:15

1 ACT 2:23 It is possible to consider προσπήγνυμι as meaning merely ‘to nail to something.’ However, in [ACT 2:23](https://ref.ly/John2:23) it is used in an absolute sense of ‘to crucify,’ and therefore can be considered as essentially equivalent in meaning to σταυρόω.

## προσπίπτω (id: 004268000000000)

προσπίπτω
=========

προσπίπτω (G4363)

aor. προσέπεσον · fem.ptc. προσπεσοῦσα \| πίπτω

17\.22 · Stances and Events Related to Stances · Prostrate as an Act of Reverence or Supplication

to prostrate oneself before, to fall down before to prostrate oneself before someone, implying supplication1 A strictly literal translation of προσπίπτω\[a] or πίπτω\[c], namely, ‘to fall down before,’ can be entirely misleading in that it may suggest an accident caused by stumbling or tripping. It may therefore be necessary in a number of languages to translate ‘to bow down low before’ or ‘to bow down to the ground before.’

[MRK 3:11](https://ref.ly/Matt3:11) · [MRK 5:33](https://ref.ly/Matt5:33) · [MRK 7:25](https://ref.ly/Matt7:25) · [LUK 5:8](https://ref.ly/Mark5:8) · [LUK 8:28](https://ref.ly/Mark8:28) · [LUK 8:47](https://ref.ly/Mark8:47) · [ACT 16:29](https://ref.ly/John16:29)

19\.11 · Physical Impact · Hit, Strike

to strike against, to hit against to strike against some object

[MAT 7:25](https://ref.ly/Matt7:25)

1 πίπτω\[c] and προσπίπτω\[a] ([17\.22](#17.22)) differ to some extent from προσκυνέω\[b] ([17\.21](#17.21)) in that they focus primarily upon the actual position rather than upon the intent of showing reverence or making supplication. προσκυνέω\[b] is the more formal term and focuses more upon the attitude of reverence or honor.

* **Associated Passages:** Mark 3:11; Mark 5:33; Mark 7:25; Luke 5:8; Luke 8:28; Luke 8:47; Acts 16:29; Matthew 7:25

59\.72 · Quantity · Add, Subtract

to add to add something to an existing quantity In a number of languages it is not at all difficult to speak of ‘adding things,’ but to add events may provide some special difficulties in restructuring. For example, in [LUK 3:20](https://ref.ly/Mark3:20) it may be important to translate as ‘he had done many other bad things, but he also did this,’ and in [GAL 2:6](https://ref.ly/2Cor2:6) it may be helpful to translate ‘for those who were of repute did not say that I should do anything more than I was doing.’

[MAT 6:27](https://ref.ly/Matt6:27) · [MRK 4:24](https://ref.ly/Matt4:24) · [LUK 3:20](https://ref.ly/Mark3:20) · [LUK 12:25](https://ref.ly/Mark12:25) · [LUK 17:5](https://ref.ly/Mark17:5) · [LUK 19:11](https://ref.ly/Mark19:11) · [LUK 20:11](https://ref.ly/Mark20:11) · [LUK 20:12](https://ref.ly/Mark20:12) · [ACT 2:41](https://ref.ly/John2:41) · [ACT 2:47](https://ref.ly/John2:47) · [ACT 5:14](https://ref.ly/John5:14) · [ACT 11:24](https://ref.ly/John11:24) · [ACT 12:3](https://ref.ly/John12:3) · [GAL 3:19](https://ref.ly/2Cor3:19) · [HEB 12:19](https://ref.ly/Phlm12:19)

## προσφάγιον (id: 004276000000000)

προσφάγιον
==========

προσφάγιον (G4371)

noun \| \-ου \| φάγος

5\.17 · Foods and Condiments · Food

fish the flesh of fish as food1 In literature outside the NT, προσφάγιον normally refers to some type of relish eaten with bread, but in [JHN 21:5](https://ref.ly/Luke21:5) (the only occurrence in the NT) the reference is to the flesh of fish.

[JHN 21:5](https://ref.ly/Luke21:5)

1 One may argue that the meaning of προσφάγιον in [JHN 21:5](https://ref.ly/Luke21:5) is relish, and that it is only the specialized context which makes possible the reference to fish. On the other hand, one may also argue that, as in the case of ὀψάριον ([5\.16](#5.16)), the term προσφάγιον has undergone a specialization of meaning, so that it no longer refers to any type of relish eaten with bread, but has the more specific meaning of fish as food.

* **Associated Passages:** John 21:5

adv \| πρόσφατος

67\.23 · Time · A Point of Time with Reference to Other Points of Time: Before, Long Ago, Now, At the Same Time, When, About, After

recently pertaining to a point of time preceding another point of time, but with a relatively short interval For languages which do not have an adverb meaning ‘recently,’ one can always introduce an equivalent approximate statement in [ACT 18:2](https://ref.ly/John18:2), for example, ‘he met a Jew who only a few days before had come from Italy.’ One cannot be certain as to the interval of time, but a phrase such as ‘a few days before’ can be a satisfactory equivalent.

[ACT 18:2](https://ref.ly/John18:2)

pouring (or possibly sprinkling) the act of pouring something on or against something Since in most contexts this act of πρόσχυσις τοῦ αἵματος is spoken of as ‘sprinkling,’ it may be preferable to employ ‘sprinkling’ in [HEB 11:28](https://ref.ly/Phlm11:28), since πρόσχυσις may have acquired a generic sense which would include the specific act of sprinkling. ## προσψαύω (id: 004284000000000)

προσψαύω
========

προσψαύω (G4379)

ψαύω (touch) · ψώχω

24\.75 · Sensory Events and States · Touch, Feel

to touch to touch something lightly, normally with the finger or hand For another interpretation of προσψαύω in [LUK 11:46](https://ref.ly/Mark11:46), see [18\.11](#18.11). Note that προσψαύω\[a] differs in meaning from προσψαύω\[b] ‘to touch’ ([18\.11](#18.11)) in that it is understood in a hyperbolic sense of ‘to merely touch,’ while προσψαύω\[b] implies ‘doing something to help’ or ‘doing something about it.’

[LUK 11:46](https://ref.ly/Mark11:46)

18\.11 · Attachment · Grasp, Hold

to touch, to touch to help to touch something, with the intent of doing something about it In employing a term for ‘touch’ in [LUK 11:46](https://ref.ly/Mark11:46), it is important to use an expression which will imply ‘touching in order to relieve the burden.’ This may be done in some instances by translating ‘to help by touching’ or ‘to touch with a finger in order to help a little.’ For another interpretation of προσψαύω in [LUK 11:46](https://ref.ly/Mark11:46), see [24\.75](#24.75).

[LUK 11:46](https://ref.ly/Mark11:46)

* **Associated Passages:** Luke 11:46

to show favoritism, to be partial, partiality to make unjust distinctions between people by treating one person better than another ‘To show favoritism’ or ‘to be partial’ is expressed in an idiomatic manner in some languages, for example, ‘to look only upon a person’s face,’ ‘to call a sparrow a chicken,’ or ‘to give one’s clansman the best piece of meat.’

88\.239 · Moral and Ethical Qualities and Related Behavior · Show Favoritism, Prejudice

one who shows favoritism, a respecter of persons (derivative of προσωπολημπτέω ‘to show favoritism,’ [88\.238](#88.238)) one who unjustly treats one person better than another

[ACT 10:34](https://ref.ly/John10:34)

* **Associated Passages:** Acts 10:34


## προσωπολημψία (id: 004287000000000)

προσωπολημψία
=============

προσωπολημψία (G4382)

noun \| \-ας \| ὁράω \+ λαμβάνω

88\.238 · Moral and Ethical Qualities and Related Behavior · Show Favoritism, Prejudice

to show favoritism, to be partial, partiality to make unjust distinctions between people by treating one person better than another ‘To show favoritism’ or ‘to be partial’ is expressed in an idiomatic manner in some languages, for example, ‘to look only upon a person’s face,’ ‘to call a sparrow a chicken,’ or ‘to give one’s clansman the best piece of meat.’

[ROM 2:11](https://ref.ly/Acts2:11) · [EPH 6:9](https://ref.ly/Gal6:9) · [COL 3:25](https://ref.ly/Phil3:25) · [JAS 2:1](https://ref.ly/Heb2:1)

* **Associated Passages:** Romans 2:11; Ephesians 6:9; Colossians 3:25; James 2:1

========

πρόσωπον (G4383)

noun \| \-ου \| ὁράω

8\.18 · Body, Body Parts, and Body Products · Parts of the Body

face the front part of the human head

[MAT 6:16](https://ref.ly/Matt6:16) · [MAT 6:17](https://ref.ly/Matt6:17) · [MAT 17:2](https://ref.ly/Matt17:2) · [MAT 17:6](https://ref.ly/Matt17:6) · [MAT 18:10](https://ref.ly/Matt18:10) · [MAT 26:39](https://ref.ly/Matt26:39) · [MAT 26:67](https://ref.ly/Matt26:67) · [MRK 14:65](https://ref.ly/Matt14:65) · [LUK 5:12](https://ref.ly/Mark5:12) · [LUK 9:29](https://ref.ly/Mark9:29) · [LUK 17:16](https://ref.ly/Mark17:16) · [LUK 24:5](https://ref.ly/Mark24:5) · [ACT 6:15](https://ref.ly/John6:15) · [ACT 6:15](https://ref.ly/John6:15) · [ACT 20:25](https://ref.ly/John20:25) · [ACT 20:38](https://ref.ly/John20:38) · [1CO 14:25](https://ref.ly/Rom14:25) · [2CO 3:7](https://ref.ly/1Cor3:7) · [2CO 3:7](https://ref.ly/1Cor3:7) · [2CO 3:13](https://ref.ly/1Cor3:13) · [2CO 3:18](https://ref.ly/1Cor3:18) · [2CO 4:6](https://ref.ly/1Cor4:6) · [2CO 11:20](https://ref.ly/1Cor11:20) · [1TH 2:17](https://ref.ly/Col2:17) · [HEB 9:24](https://ref.ly/Phlm9:24) · [JAS 1:23](https://ref.ly/Heb1:23) · [REV 4:7](https://ref.ly/Jude4:7) · [REV 6:16](https://ref.ly/Jude6:16) · [REV 7:11](https://ref.ly/Jude7:11) · [REV 9:7](https://ref.ly/Jude9:7) · [REV 9:7](https://ref.ly/Jude9:7) · [REV 10:1](https://ref.ly/Jude10:1) · [REV 11:16](https://ref.ly/Jude11:16) · [REV 22:4](https://ref.ly/Jude22:4)

9\.9 · People · Human Beings

person, individual (a figurative extension of meaning of πρόσωπον\[a] face, [8\.18](#8.18))

[LUK 9:53](https://ref.ly/Mark9:53) · [ACT 20:25](https://ref.ly/John20:25) · [2CO 1:11](https://ref.ly/1Cor1:11) · [GAL 1:22](https://ref.ly/2Cor1:22) · [COL 2:1](https://ref.ly/Phil2:1) · [1TH 2:17](https://ref.ly/Col2:17) · [1PE 3:12](https://ref.ly/Jas3:12)

79\.93 · Features of Objects · Two\-Dimensional and Three\-Dimensional Shapes

surface, face the two\-dimensional surface of an object In a number of cases one does not translate πρόσωπον in the sense of ‘surface’ since it is an implied component of other terms. For example, the phrase ‘on the earth’ implies the surface of the earth.

[LUK 21:35](https://ref.ly/Mark21:35) · [ACT 17:26](https://ref.ly/John17:26)

24\.24 · Sensory Events and States · See

appearance the form or characteristics of something as seen

[MAT 16:3](https://ref.ly/Matt16:3) · [LUK 12:56](https://ref.ly/Mark12:56) · [2CO 5:12](https://ref.ly/1Cor5:12) · [JAS 1:11](https://ref.ly/Heb1:11)

85\.26 · Existence in Space · Be in a Place

presence, being at a place (a figurative extension of meaning of πρόσωπον\[a] ‘face,’ [8\.18](#8.18)) the personal presence of an individual at a particular place

[LUK 2:31](https://ref.ly/Mark2:31) · [ACT 2:28](https://ref.ly/John2:28) · [ACT 3:13](https://ref.ly/John3:13) · [ACT 3:20](https://ref.ly/John3:20) · [ACT 5:41](https://ref.ly/John5:41) · [ACT 25:16](https://ref.ly/John25:16) · [2CO 2:10](https://ref.ly/1Cor2:10) · [2CO 8:24](https://ref.ly/1Cor8:24) · [2CO 10:1](https://ref.ly/1Cor10:1) · [GAL 2:11](https://ref.ly/2Cor2:11) · [1TH 2:17](https://ref.ly/Col2:17) · [1TH 2:17](https://ref.ly/Col2:17) · [1TH 3:10](https://ref.ly/Col3:10) · [2TH 1:9](https://ref.ly/1Thess1:9) · [HEB 9:24](https://ref.ly/Phlm9:24) · [REV 12:14](https://ref.ly/Jude12:14) · [REV 20:11](https://ref.ly/Jude20:11)

83\.33 · Spacial Positions · In Front Of, Face To Face, In Back Of, Behind

in front of, before a position in front of an object, whether animate or inanimate, which is regarded as having a spacial orientation of front and back1

[MRK 1:2](https://ref.ly/Matt1:2) · [LUK 10:1](https://ref.ly/Mark10:1) · [ACT 7:45](https://ref.ly/John7:45)

83\.37 · Spacial Positions · In Front Of, Face To Face, In Back Of, Behind

**Collocation:** πρόσωπον πρὸς πρόσωπον

face to face (an idiom, literally: face to face) the position of one person facing another, with the implication of direct, personal interaction The implication of this idiom in [1CO 13:12](https://ref.ly/Rom13:12) is that there will be clear understanding, and in some languages it is necessary to shift somewhat the figurative expression. For example, it may be necessary to render this passage as ‘how we now understand is like seeing a dim image in a mirror, but then we shall understand as clearly as though we were seeing face to face’ or ‘... as though we were seeing something directly.’

[1CO 13:12](https://ref.ly/Rom13:12) · [1CO 13:12](https://ref.ly/Rom13:12)

30\.120 · Think · To Distinguish, To Evaluate, To Judge

**Collocation:** βλέπω εἰς πρόσωπον

to judge on the basis of appearance, to render a superficial judgment, to pay no attention to a person’s status, to judge on the basis of reputation (an idiom, literally: to see into the face) to judge on the basis of external appearances In some languages an equivalent of this expression in [MAT 22:16](https://ref.ly/Matt22:16) may be ‘when you judge, you look into a man’s heart’ or ‘when you judge you see more than a person’s face’ or ‘when you judge a person, who he appears to be doesn’t count.’

[MAT 22:16](https://ref.ly/Matt22:16) · [MRK 12:14](https://ref.ly/Matt12:14)

67\.19 · Time · A Point of Time with Reference to Other Points of Time: Before, Long Ago, Now, At the Same Time, When, About, After

**Collocation:** πρὸ προσώπου

before, previous (an idiom, literally: before the face, equivalent in meaning to πρό\[b] ‘before,’ [67\.17](#67.17), but a somewhat more elaborate phrase rhetorically) a point of time, possibly only a short time before another point of time

[MAT 11:10](https://ref.ly/Matt11:10) · [LUK 7:27](https://ref.ly/Mark7:27) · [LUK 9:52](https://ref.ly/Mark9:52) · [ACT 13:24](https://ref.ly/John13:24)

30\.80 · Think · To Decide, To Conclude

**Collocation:** στηρίζω τὸ πρόσωπον

to decide firmly, to resolve, to make up one’s mind definitely (a Semitic idiom, literally: to fix one’s face) to make a decision, with emphasis upon finality In order to express the meaning of ‘to decide firmly,’ it may be useful in some instances to translate ‘to decide and to refuse to change one’s mind’ or ‘to decide and not to change.’

[LUK 9:51](https://ref.ly/Mark9:51)

31\.31 · Hold a View, Believe, Trust · Suppose, Think Possible

**Collocation:** τὰ κατὰ πρόσωπον

outward appearance, what things seem to be (an idiom, literally: those things according to the face) that which seems to be so

[2CO 10:7](https://ref.ly/1Cor10:7)

88\.238 · Moral and Ethical Qualities and Related Behavior · Show Favoritism, Prejudice

**Collocation:** λαμβάνω πρόσωπον

to show favoritism, to be partial, partiality (an idiom, literally: to take face) to make unjust distinctions between people by treating one person better than another ‘To show favoritism’ or ‘to be partial’ is expressed in an idiomatic manner in some languages, for example, ‘to look only upon a person’s face,’ ‘to call a sparrow a chicken,’ or ‘to give one’s clansman the best piece of meat.’

[LUK 20:21](https://ref.ly/Mark20:21) · [GAL 2:6](https://ref.ly/2Cor2:6)

33\.365 · Communication · Flatter

**Collocation:** θαυμάζω πρόσωπον

to flatter (an idiom, literally: to admire the face) to praise someone, normally in an exaggerated or false manner and with insincere purpose2

[JUD 1:16](https://ref.ly/3John1:16)

1 There are no doubt certain slight differences of meaning in this series ([83\.33](#83.33)), but such distinctions cannot be readily determined from existing contexts. 2 It is not possible to determine precisely the context from which this idiom is derived, but it would appear that the meaning to flatter is probably the most satisfactory.

πρότερος
========

adj \| \-α, \-ον \| πρό

67\.18 · Time · A Point of Time with Reference to Other Points of Time: Before, Long Ago, Now, At the Same Time, When, About, After

before, former, formerly pertaining to a point of time earlier in a sequence1

[JHN 6:62](https://ref.ly/Luke6:62) · [JHN 7:50](https://ref.ly/Luke7:50) · [JHN 9:8](https://ref.ly/Luke9:8) · [2CO 1:15](https://ref.ly/1Cor1:15) · [EPH 4:22](https://ref.ly/Gal4:22) · [1TI 1:13](https://ref.ly/2Thess1:13) · [HEB 4:6](https://ref.ly/Phlm4:6) · [HEB 10:32](https://ref.ly/Phlm10:32) · [1PE 1:14](https://ref.ly/Jas1:14)

60\.47 · Number · First, Second, Third, Etc. \[Ordinals]

the first time first, with the implication of emphasis, frequently in reference to time It is also possible to understand πρότερος in [GAL 4:13](https://ref.ly/2Cor4:13) as meaning ‘earlier, formerly’ (see [67\.18](#67.18)).

[2CO 1:15](https://ref.ly/1Cor1:15) · [GAL 4:13](https://ref.ly/2Cor4:13) · [HEB 7:27](https://ref.ly/Phlm7:27)

1 It would be possible to classify meanings such as before and former as a matter of sequence (Domain 61\), but the meanings in this series do not point to the first item in a sequence but simply to a point of time prior to some other point.

1 προτίθεμαι\[a] is similar in meaning to βούλομαι\[b] and βουλεύομαι\[a] ([30\.56](#30.56)) with perhaps, however, some additional emphasis upon prior planning. βούλομαι\[b] and βουλεύομαι\[a] also imply some prior thought by virtue of their meaning to plan.

## προφήτης (id: 004299000000000)

προφήτης
========

προφήτης (G4396) — προφῆται

noun \| \-ου \| φημί

53\.79 · Religious Activities · Roles and Functions

prophet, inspired preacher one who proclaims inspired utterances on behalf of God1 There is a tendency in a number of languages to translate προφήτης only in the sense of ‘one who foretells the future,’ but foretelling the future was only a relatively minor aspect of the prophet’s function, though gradually it became more important. Patristic authors defined the function of a prophet mainly in terms of foretelling the future. In New Testament times, however, the focus was upon the inspired utterance proclaimed on behalf of and on the authority of God. Accordingly, in a number of languages it is more appropriate to translate προφήτης as ‘one who speaks for God.’

[MAT 1:22](https://ref.ly/Matt1:22) · [MAT 2:5](https://ref.ly/Matt2:5) · [MAT 2:15](https://ref.ly/Matt2:15) · [MAT 2:17](https://ref.ly/Matt2:17) · [MAT 2:23](https://ref.ly/Matt2:23) · [MAT
