18:20](https://ref.ly/Jude18:20) · [REV 18:24](https://ref.ly/Jude18:24) · [REV 22:6](https://ref.ly/Jude22:6) · [REV 22:9](https://ref.ly/Jude22:9)

33\.60 · ((occurring in the plural as a collective)) · Communication · Written Language

**Collocation:** προφῆται

the writings of the prophets, the Prophets the writings of the prophets, including both the earlier and the later prophets2 It is quite impossible in many languages to speak of ‘the Prophets’ in reference to the writings of the prophets, and therefore one must often say ‘what the prophets wrote’ or ‘the books containing the words of the prophets.’

[JHN 6:45](https://ref.ly/Luke6:45) · [ACT 10:43](https://ref.ly/John10:43) · [ACT 15:15](https://ref.ly/John15:15) · [ACT 24:14](https://ref.ly/John24:14) · [ACT 26:22](https://ref.ly/John26:22) · [ACT 26:27](https://ref.ly/John26:27) · [ROM 1:2](https://ref.ly/Acts1:2) · [1PE 1:10](https://ref.ly/Jas1:10)

33\.58 · Communication · Written Language

**Collocation:** ὁ νόμος καὶ οἱ προφῆται

the sacred writings, the Law and the Prophets (an idiom, literally: the Law and the Prophets) all of the sacred writings of the OT, including the Law, the Prophets, and the Writings3

[MAT 5:17](https://ref.ly/Matt5:17) · [MAT 7:12](https://ref.ly/Matt7:12) · [MAT 11:13](https://ref.ly/Matt11:13) · [MAT 22:40](https://ref.ly/Matt22:40) · [ACT 13:15](https://ref.ly/John13:15) · [ACT 28:23](https://ref.ly/John28:23) · [ROM 3:21](https://ref.ly/Acts3:21)

1 Even in the NT, the role of the προφήτης had become recognized as a special function, and therefore it should not be equated with preaching ([33\.256](#33.256)\-259\) in general. 2 It is possible that προφῆται may have an even wider range of meaning, as in [LUK 24:25](https://ref.ly/Mark24:25) (ὦ ἀνόητοι καὶ βραδεῖς τῇ καρδίᾳ τοῦ πιστεύειν ἐπὶ πᾶσιν οἷς ἐλάλησαν οἱ προφῆται ‘how foolish you are and how slow you are to believe everything the prophets said’). 3 The phrase ὁ νόμος καὶ οἱ προφῆται is classified as an idiom, since the meaning of this fixed phrase is really different from the total of the constituent parts, in that more is included than merely the Law and the Prophets.

* **Associated Passages:** Matthew 1:22; Matthew 2:5; Matthew 2:15; Matthew 2:17; Matthew 2:23; Matthew 3:3; Matthew 4:14; Matthew 5:12; Matthew 5:17; Matthew 7:12; Matthew 8:17; Matthew 10:41; Matthew 11:9; Matthew 11:13; Matthew 12:17; Matthew 12:39; Matthew 13:17; Matthew 13:35; Matthew 13:57; Matthew 14:5; Matthew 16:14; Matthew 21:4; Matthew 21:11; Matthew 21:26; Matthew 21:46; Matthew 22:40; Matthew 23:29; Matthew 23:30; Matthew 23:31; Matthew 23:34; Matthew 23:37; Matthew 24:15; Matthew 26:56; Matthew 27:9; Mark 1:2; Mark 6:4; Mark 6:15; Mark 8:28; Mark 11:32; Luke 1:70; Luke 1:76; Luke 3:4; Luke 4:17; Luke 4:24; Luke 4:27; Luke 6:23; Luke 7:16; Luke 7:26; Luke 7:39; Luke 9:8; Luke 9:19; Luke 10:24; Luke 11:47; Luke 11:49; Luke 11:50; Luke 13:28; Luke 13:33; Luke 13:34; Luke 16:16; Luke 16:29; Luke 16:31; Luke 18:31; Luke 20:6; Luke 24:19; Luke 24:25; Luke 24:27; Luke 24:44; John 1:21; John 1:23; John 1:25; John 1:45; John 4:19; John 4:44; John 6:14; John 6:45; John 7:40; John 7:52; John 8:52; John 8:53; John 9:17; John 12:38; Acts 2:16; Acts 2:30; Acts 3:18; Acts 3:21; Acts 3:22; Acts 3:23; Acts 3:24; Acts 3:25; Acts 7:37; Acts 7:42; Acts 7:48; Acts 7:52; Acts 8:28; Acts 8:30; Acts 8:34; Acts 10:43; Acts 11:27; Acts 13:1; Acts 13:15; Acts 13:20; Acts 13:27; Acts 13:40; Acts 15:15; Acts 15:32; Acts 21:10; Acts 24:14; Acts 26:22; Acts 26:27; Acts 28:23; Acts 28:25; Romans 1:2; Romans 3:21; Romans 11:3; 1 Corinthians 12:28; 1 Corinthians 12:29; 1 Corinthians 14:29; 1 Corinthians 14:32; 1 Corinthians 14:37; Ephesians 2:20; Ephesians 3:5; Ephesians 4:11; 1 Thessalonians 2:15; Titus 1:12; Hebrews 1:1; Hebrews 11:32; James 5:10; 1 Peter 1:10; 2 Peter 2:16; 2 Peter 3:2; Revelation 10:7; Revelation 11:10; Revelation 11:18; Revelation 16:6; Revelation 18:20; Revelation 18:24; Revelation 22:6; Revelation 22:9

to be ahead of, to anticipate, to be in advance to do something at a point of time which immediately precedes another point of time It would also be possible to render this expression in [MAT 17:25](https://ref.ly/Matt17:25) as ‘Jesus spoke to Simon before Simon could speak to him.’

## πρωϊνός (id: 004309000000000)

πρωϊνός
=======

πρωϊνός (G4407)

adj \| \-ή, \-όν \| πρωτεύω

67\.188 · Time · Definite Units of Time: Year, Month, Week, Day, Hour

of the early morning pertaining to early morning

[REV 2:28](https://ref.ly/Jude2:28) · [REV 22:16](https://ref.ly/Jude22:16)

1\.33 · Geographical Objects and Features · Heavenly Bodies

**Collocation:** ἀστὴρ πρωϊνός

morning star, bright star in the morning sky (an idiom, literally: morning star) a planet which is conspicuous in the morning sky (usually either Venus or Mars) In [REV 2:28](https://ref.ly/Jude2:28) the singular pronoun αὐτῷ refers to any and all of the persons in Thyatira, and therefore it is usually best rendered as plural. A literal rendering of [REV 2:28](https://ref.ly/Jude2:28) may be confusing, since it would suggest that God would be handing over the morning star to persons in Thyatira. A more satisfactory rendering may be ‘I will cause the morning star to shine upon them’ or possibly ‘I will bring light into their hearts.’

[REV 2:28](https://ref.ly/Jude2:28) · [REV 22:16](https://ref.ly/Jude22:16)

* **Associated Passages:** Revelation 2:28; Revelation 22:16


## πρῷρα (id: 004310000000000)

πρῷρα
=====

πρῷρα (G4408)

noun \| \-ης \| πρωτεύω

6\.46 · Artifacts · Boats and Parts of Boats

bow the forepart of a boat In a number of languages there are rather technical terms for the bow and stern of a boat, but where such terms are lacking, one can speak of ‘the front of a boat’ for πρῷρα and ‘the back of the boat’ for πρύμνα ([6\.47](#6.47)).

[ACT 27:30](https://ref.ly/John27:30) · [ACT 27:41](https://ref.ly/John27:41)

* **Associated Passages:** Acts 27:30; Acts 27:41

before, former, formerly pertaining to a point of time earlier in a sequence1

87\.45 · Status · High Status or Rank \[including persons of high status]

great, prominent, important, foremost pertaining to being of high rank, with the implication of special prominence and status

[MAT 19:30](https://ref.ly/Matt19:30) · [MAT 19:30](https://ref.ly/Matt19:30) · [MAT 20:16](https://ref.ly/Matt20:16) · [MAT 20:16](https://ref.ly/Matt20:16) · [MAT 20:27](https://ref.ly/Matt20:27) · [MRK 6:21](https://ref.ly/Matt6:21) · [MRK 10:31](https://ref.ly/Matt10:31) · [MRK 10:31](https://ref.ly/Matt10:31) · [MRK 10:44](https://ref.ly/Matt10:44) · [LUK 13:30](https://ref.ly/Mark13:30) · [LUK 13:30](https://ref.ly/Mark13:30) · [LUK 19:47](https://ref.ly/Mark19:47) · [ACT 13:50](https://ref.ly/John13:50) · [ACT 17:4](https://ref.ly/John17:4) · [ACT 25:2](https://ref.ly/John25:2) · [ACT 28:7](https://ref.ly/John28:7) · [ACT 28:17](https://ref.ly/John28:17) · [1TI 1:15](https://ref.ly/2Thess1:15) · [1TI 1:16](https://ref.ly/2Thess1:16)

65\.24 · Value · Good, Bad

best pertaining to being superior in value to all other items of the same class

[LUK 15:22](https://ref.ly/Mark15:22)

65\.52 · Value · Important, Unimportant

most important pertaining to exceeding everything else in importance In some languages ‘most important’ is expressed by a negative comparison, for example, ‘the commandment that nothing else surpasses’ or ‘the commandment than which nothing else is greater’ or ‘... of more value.’

[MAT 22:38](https://ref.ly/Matt22:38) · [MRK 9:35](https://ref.ly/Matt9:35) · [MRK 12:28](https://ref.ly/Matt12:28) · [MRK 12:29](https://ref.ly/Matt12:29) · [ROM 3:2](https://ref.ly/Acts3:2) · [1CO 15:3](https://ref.ly/Rom15:3)

birthright, rights of being the firstborn rights associated with being the firstborn

[HEB 12:16](https://ref.ly/Phlm12:16)

## πρωτότοκος (id: 004317000000000)

πρωτότοκος
==========

πρωτότοκος (G4416)

adj \| \-ον \| πρωτεύω \+ τίκτω

10\.43 · Kinship Terms · Kinship Relations Involving Successive Generations

firstborn pertaining to being a firstborn child (normally in contexts speaking of people but also used in reference to domestic animals) In Jewish society the rights and responsibilities of being a firstborn son resulted in considerable prestige and status. The firstborn son, for example, received twice as much in inheritance as any other offspring.  
The use of πρωτότοκος ‘firstborn’ does not imply in Greek that other children were also born to a woman, though in a number of languages one would never use ‘firstborn’ unless other children followed. Such an individual would be spoken of merely as ‘the only child.’ It is also frequently necessary to employ an appropriate qualifier for ‘firstborn’ in order to mark clearly the fact that it is ‘a firstborn son’ rather than ‘a firstborn daughter.’  
The figurative meaning of πρωτότοκος in the messianic title πρωτότοκος πάσης κτίσεως ‘firstborn of all creation’ ([COL 1:15](https://ref.ly/Phil1:15) may be interpreted as ‘existing before all creation’ (see [13\.79](#13.79)) or ‘existing superior to all creation’ (see [87\.47](#87.47)).

[LUK 2:7](https://ref.ly/Mark2:7) · [COL 1:15](https://ref.ly/Phil1:15) · [COL 1:18](https://ref.ly/Phil1:18) · [HEB 1:6](https://ref.ly/Phlm1:6) · [HEB 11:28](https://ref.ly/Phlm11:28) · [HEB 12:23](https://ref.ly/Phlm12:23) · [REV 1:5](https://ref.ly/Jude1:5)

13\.79 · Be, Become, Exist, Happen · Exist

existing first, existing before pertaining to existing prior to something else It is possible to understand πρωτότοκος in [COL 1:15](https://ref.ly/Phil1:15) as ‘superior in status’ (see [87\.47](#87.47)). See also discussion at [10\.43](#10.43).

[ROM 8:29](https://ref.ly/Acts8:29) · [COL 1:15](https://ref.ly/Phil1:15)

87\.47 · Status · High Status or Rank \[including persons of high status]

superior to, above all pertaining to being superior to everything else in the same or related class For another interpretation of πρωτότοκος in [COL 1:15](https://ref.ly/Phil1:15), see [13\.79](#13.79); see also discussion at [10\.43](#10.43).

[ROM 8:29](https://ref.ly/Acts8:29) · [COL 1:15](https://ref.ly/Phil1:15) · [COL 1:18](https://ref.ly/Phil1:18)

* **Associated Passages:** Luke 2:7; Colossians 1:15; Colossians 1:18; Hebrews 1:6; Hebrews 11:28; Hebrews 12:23; Revelation 1:5; Romans 8:29

[ROM 11:11](https://ref.ly/Acts11:11) · [JAS 2:10](https://ref.ly/Heb2:10) · [JAS 3:2](https://ref.ly/Heb3:2) · [JAS 3:2](https://ref.ly/Heb3:2) · [2PE 1:10](https://ref.ly/1Pet1:10)

## πτέρνα (id: 004320000000000)

πτέρνα
======

πτέρνα (G4418)

noun \| \-ης

8\.52 · Body, Body Parts, and Body Products · Parts of the Body

heel the back part of the human foot below the ankle πτέρνα occurs in the NT only in [JHN 13:18](https://ref.ly/Luke13:18) (ὁ τρώγων μου τὸν ἄρτον ἐπῆρεν ἐπ ̓ ἐμὲ τὴν πτέρναν αὐτοῦ ‘the one eating my bread has raised his heel against me’) in an idiomatic expression denoting antagonism and opposition (see [39\.3](#39.3)).

[JHN 13:18](https://ref.ly/Luke13:18)

39\.3 · Hostility, Strife · Opposition, Hostility

**Collocation:** ἐπαίρω τὴν πτέρναν

to turn against, to oppose (an idiom, literally: to lift one’s heel against) to oppose someone by turning against such a person, possibly with focus upon the initial aspect of becoming opposed to In a number of languages a convenient way of rendering ‘to turn against’ may be ‘to become an enemy of.’  
πτέρνα occurs in the NT only in [JHN 13:18](https://ref.ly/Luke13:18) in an idiomatic expression denoting antagonism and opposition.

[JHN 13:18](https://ref.ly/Luke13:18)

* **Associated Passages:** John 13:18


## πτερύγιον (id: 004321000000000)

πτερύγιον
=========

πτερύγιον (G4419)

noun \| \-ου \| πτέρυξ

7\.53 · Constructions · Parts and Areas of Buildings

pinnacle, summit (of the Temple) the tip or high point of a building1

[MAT 4:5](https://ref.ly/Matt4:5) · [LUK 4:9](https://ref.ly/Mark4:9)

1 The precise meaning of πτερύγιον in [MAT 4:5](https://ref.ly/Matt4:5) and [LUK 4:9](https://ref.ly/Mark4:9) is uncertain. Some scholars have proposed lintel or superstructure of the gate of the Temple.

* **Associated Passages:** Matthew 4:5; Luke 4:9


## πτέρυξ (id: 004322000000000)

πτέρυξ
======

πτέρυξ (G4420)

noun \| \-υγος \| πτερύγιον

8\.29 · Body, Body Parts, and Body Products · Parts of the Body

wing a wing of any flying creature In [REV 9:9](https://ref.ly/Jude9:9), in which the apocalyptic locusts are mentioned, the expression ἡ φωνὴ τῶν πτερύγων may require a distinct term for ‘wings,’ since the wings of insects may be referred to by terms different from those employed in speaking of the wings of birds.

[MAT 23:37](https://ref.ly/Matt23:37) · [LUK 13:34](https://ref.ly/Mark13:34) · [REV 4:8](https://ref.ly/Jude4:8) · [REV 9:9](https://ref.ly/Jude9:9) · [REV 12:14](https://ref.ly/Jude12:14)

## πτηνός (id: 004323000000000)

πτηνός
======

πτηνός (G4421) — πτηνόν

adj \| (\-ή), \-όν \| πέτομαι

4\.38 · Animals · Birds

bird any kind of bird, wild or domestic All languages have at least some terms for birds, but there may be no generic term which identifies birds while excluding all other creatures which fly. For example, in some languages a term which includes all kinds of birds also includes flying insects and bats. However, since in most instances such a term has birds as its primary reference, it can usually be employed in contexts in which birds are more or less readily identified by other expressions in the passage. In other languages generic terms for birds may identify only certain classes of birds, for example, birds of prey, water birds, and birds of the forest.

[1CO 15:39](https://ref.ly/Rom15:39)

* **Associated Passages:** 1 Corinthians 15:39

noun \| \-ου \| πτύω

a winnowing shovel a fork\-like shovel for throwing threshed grain into the air so that the wind may separate the chaff from the grain Where there is no receptor\-language term for a winnowing shovel, one may use a descriptive phrase, for example, ‘a tool for throwing threshed grain into the air in order to let the chaff blow away.’

πτύσσω
======

πτύσσω (G4428)

aor.ptc. πτύξας \| ἀναπτύσσω

to roll up, to make into a roll to cause something to be in the shape of a roll1

[LUK 4:20](https://ref.ly/Mark4:20)

## πτύω (id: 004331000000000)

πτύω
====

πτύω (G4429)

ἐκπτύω · ἐμπτύω · πτύον · πτύσμα

23\.43 · Physiological Processes and States · Processes Involving the Mouth, Other Than Eating and Drinking

to spit to spit on or at something or someone1 In some societies spitting has quite a different symbolic meaning than it has in the Western world. In fact, in some societies in Africa, to spit upon a person’s head is to confer a blessing. It may therefore be necessary in the case of [MAT 26:67](https://ref.ly/Matt26:67) to translate ‘they spat in his face’ as ‘they spat in his face to dishonor him’ or ‘... to show their anger against him.’

[MRK 7:33](https://ref.ly/Matt7:33) · [MRK 8:23](https://ref.ly/Matt8:23) · [JHN 9:6](https://ref.ly/Luke9:6)

1 It is possible that a distinction in meaning should be made between πτύω and ἐμπτύω, in which case the latter term would emphasize the spitting in or upon an object. However, there are insufficient contrasts in contexts to warrant making such a distinction.

* **Associated Passages:** Mark 7:33; Mark 8:23; John 9:6


## πτῶμα (id: 004332000000000)

πτῶμα
=====

πτῶμα (G4430)

noun \| \-τος \| πίπτω

8\.7 · Body, Body Parts, and Body Products · Body

dead body, corpse a dead body, whether of an animal or a human being In the parallel to [MAT 24:28](https://ref.ly/Matt24:28) ([LUK 17:37](https://ref.ly/Mark17:37)), the term σῶμα ([8\.1](#8.1)) occurs. Since in some languages one must distinguish varying degrees of decomposition of a corpse, it may be important in certain contexts to indicate whether the body is of a person or an animal which has recently died or one which has undergone considerable decomposition.

[MAT 14:12](https://ref.ly/Matt14:12) · [MAT 24:28](https://ref.ly/Matt24:28) · [MRK 6:29](https://ref.ly/Matt6:29) · [MRK 15:45](https://ref.ly/Matt15:45) · [REV 11:8](https://ref.ly/Jude11:8) · [REV 11:9](https://ref.ly/Jude11:9) · [REV 11:9](https://ref.ly/Jude11:9)

* **Associated Passages:** Matthew 14:12; Matthew 24:28; Mark 6:29; Mark 15:45; Revelation 11:8; Revelation 11:9


## πτῶσις (id: 004333000000000)

πτῶσις
======

πτῶσις (G4431)

noun \| \-εως \| πίπτω

20\.50 · Violence, Harm, Destroy, Kill · Destroy

destruction (a figurative extension of meaning of πτῶσις ‘fall,’ not occurring in the NT) to suffer destruction or ruin, with the implication of having formerly held a position of eminence In rendering εἰς πτῶσιν καὶ ἀνάστασιν πολλῶν in [LUK 2:34](https://ref.ly/Mark2:34) it is important to indicate clearly that some are to be destroyed and others are to rise. If one translates literally ‘for the destruction and rise of many,’ it might imply that certain individuals are to be destroyed and then to rise again. For other interpretations of this passage, see [13\.59](#13.59) and [87\.75](#87.75).

[MAT 7:27](https://ref.ly/Matt7:27) · [LUK 2:34](https://ref.ly/Mark2:34)

87\.75 · Status · Low Status or Rank \[including persons of low status]

a falling (a figurative extension of meaning of πτῶσις ‘fall,’ not occurring in the NT) radical change toward a lower status (note the contrast with ἀνάστασις\[b] rising up, [87\.39](#87.39)) For somewhat different interpretations of πτῶσις in [LUK 2:34](https://ref.ly/Mark2:34), see [20\.50](#20.50) and [13\.59](#13.59).

[LUK 2:34](https://ref.ly/Mark2:34)

13\.59 · Be, Become, Exist, Happen · Change of State

to fall from, to worsen to change for the worse, with emphasis upon extent and suddenness For somewhat different interpretations of πτῶσις in [LUK 2:34](https://ref.ly/Mark2:34), see [20\.50](#20.50) and [87\.75](#87.75).

[LUK 2:34](https://ref.ly/Mark2:34)

* **Associated Passages:** Matthew 7:27; Luke 2:34

πτωχεία
=======

πτωχεία (G4432)

noun \| \-ας \| πτωχεύω

57\.52 · Possess, Transfer, Exchange · Be Poor, Be Needy, Poverty

poverty, destitution a state of having insufficient possessions

[2CO 8:2](https://ref.ly/1Cor8:2) · [2CO 8:9](https://ref.ly/1Cor8:9) · [REV 2:9](https://ref.ly/Jude2:9)

* **Associated Passages:** 2 Corinthians 8:2; 2 Corinthians 8:9; Revelation 2:9


## πτωχεύω (id: 004335000000000)

πτωχεύω
=======

πτωχεύω (G4433)

πτωχεία · πτωχός

57\.54 · Possess, Transfer, Exchange · Be Poor, Be Needy, Poverty

to become poor to change to a state of poverty1 In some languages it may be inappropriate to translate literally ‘rich’ and ‘poor,’ since these may have highly specific meanings and inappropriate connotations for the context of [2CO 8:9](https://ref.ly/1Cor8:9). Therefore, it may be necessary to translate ‘although Christ had previously possessed very much, he became one who possessed nothing and he did this for your sake.’

[2CO 8:9](https://ref.ly/1Cor8:9)

1 Outside of NT usage πτωχεύω means simply to be poor, but in the NT πτωχεύω is used only figuratively in referring to Christ’s incarnation.

* **Associated Passages:** 2 Corinthians 8:9


## πτωχός (id: 004336000000000)

πτωχός
======

πτωχός (G4434)

adj \| \-ή, \-όν \| πτωχεύω

57\.53 · Possess, Transfer, Exchange · Be Poor, Be Needy, Poverty

poor, destitute pertaining to being poor and destitute, implying a continuous state See comments at [57\.51](#57.51).  
Since in all societies there are poor people, there is no difficulty involved in finding a suitable expression to designate such persons, but in some languages idiomatic phrases are used, for example, ‘those who walk in rags’ or ‘those whose ribs are always showing.’

[MAT 11:5](https://ref.ly/Matt11:5) · [MAT 19:21](https://ref.ly/Matt19:21) · [MAT 26:9](https://ref.ly/Matt26:9) · [MAT 26:11](https://ref.ly/Matt26:11) · [MRK 10:21](https://ref.ly/Matt10:21) · [MRK 12:42](https://ref.ly/Matt12:42) · [MRK 12:43](https://ref.ly/Matt12:43) · [MRK 14:5](https://ref.ly/Matt14:5) · [MRK 14:7](https://ref.ly/Matt14:7) · [LUK 4:18](https://ref.ly/Mark4:18) · [LUK 6:20](https://ref.ly/Mark6:20) · [LUK 7:22](https://ref.ly/Mark7:22) · [LUK 14:13](https://ref.ly/Mark14:13) · [LUK 14:21](https://ref.ly/Mark14:21) · [LUK 16:20](https://ref.ly/Mark16:20) · [LUK 16:22](https://ref.ly/Mark16:22) · [LUK 18:22](https://ref.ly/Mark18:22) · [LUK 19:8](https://ref.ly/Mark19:8) · [LUK 21:3](https://ref.ly/Mark21:3) · [JHN 12:5](https://ref.ly/Luke12:5) · [JHN 12:6](https://ref.ly/Luke12:6) · [JHN 12:8](https://ref.ly/Luke12:8) · [JHN 13:29](https://ref.ly/Luke13:29) · [ROM 15:26](https://ref.ly/Acts15:26) · [2CO 6:10](https://ref.ly/1Cor6:10) · [GAL 2:10](https://ref.ly/2Cor2:10) · [JAS 2:2](https://ref.ly/Heb2:2) · [JAS 2:3](https://ref.ly/Heb2:3) · [JAS 2:5](https://ref.ly/Heb2:5) · [JAS 2:6](https://ref.ly/Heb2:6) · [REV 3:17](https://ref.ly/Jude3:17) · [REV 13:16](https://ref.ly/Jude13:16)

65\.16 · Value · Valuable, Lacking in Value

of little or no value, relatively worthless pertaining to being of inadequate or insufficient value It is possible that πτωχός in [GAL 4:9](https://ref.ly/2Cor4:9) refers to the contrast with that which is beneficial or useful, in which case the meaning of πτωχός in this context would be classified in Subdomain D Useful, Useless (Domain 65 Value).

[GAL 4:9](https://ref.ly/2Cor4:9)

88\.57 · Moral and Ethical Qualities and Related Behavior · Humility

**Collocation:** πτωχὸς τῷ πνεύματι

to be humble (an idiom, literally: poor in spirit) pertaining to one who is humble with regard to his own capacities (in the one NT occurrence, namely, [MAT 5:3](https://ref.ly/Matt5:3), this humility is in relationship to God) A literal translation of πτωχὸς τῷ πνεύματι may lead to serious misunderstanding, since ‘poor in spirit’ is likely to mean either ‘lacking in the Holy Spirit’ or ‘lacking in ambition or drive.’ In order to indicate clearly that this poverty or need is related in some way to spiritual realities, one may translate ‘happy are those who recognize their need of God.’

[MAT 5:3](https://ref.ly/Matt5:3)

## πυγμή (id: 004337000000000)

πυγμή
=====

πυγμή (G4435)

noun \| \-ῆς

8\.35 · Body, Body Parts, and Body Products · Parts of the Body

fist the clenched hand In the NT πυγμή occurs only in this very problematic expression in [MRK 7:3](https://ref.ly/Matt7:3). Some manuscripts omit πυγμῇ, while others read πυκνά ‘often.’ A number of translators have attempted to solve the interpretative problem in [MRK 7:3](https://ref.ly/Matt7:3) by translating ‘unless they wash in the proper manner’ or ‘... thoroughly.’

[MRK 7:3](https://ref.ly/Matt7:3)

noun nom \| \-ου

## πύθων (id: 004339000000000)

πύθων
=====

πύθων (G4436)

noun \| \-ωνος

12\.48 · Supernatural Beings and Powers · Supernatural Powers

spirit of divination a supernatural power of divination known as ‘Python’ For πύθων as part of an idiom, see [33\.285](#33.285).  
In most languages there seems to be no reason to borrow the term ‘Python,’ since it may be readily misunderstood. It is both more meaningful and to some extent more accurate to translate ‘a spirit of divination’ or ‘the spirit which caused her to foretell the future’ or ‘... to tell what was going to happen.’

[ACT 16:16](https://ref.ly/John16:16)

## πυκνός (id: 004340000000000)

πυκνός
======

πυκνός (G4437) — πυκνότερον

adj \| \-ή, \-ον \| πυκτεύω

67\.12 · Time · A Point of Time without Reference to Other Points of Time: Time, Occasion, Ever, Often

often, frequent, so often pertaining to a number of related points of time occurring with limited intervals

[LUK 5:33](https://ref.ly/Mark5:33) · [1TI 5:23](https://ref.ly/2Thess5:23)

67\.13 · (πυκνότερον) · Time · A Point of Time without Reference to Other Points of Time: Time, Occasion, Ever, Often

so often, very often, as often as possible pertaining to a number of related points of time occurring with short intervals and probably somewhat more emphatic than πυκνός, πυκνά ([67\.12](#67.12)) It is possible to understand πυκνότερον in [ACT 24:26](https://ref.ly/John24:26) as a comparative form of the stem πυκνο, but it is also possible that any comparative force may have been lost and thus πυκνότερον would not be significantly different in meaning from πυκνά ([67\.12](#67.12)).

[ACT 24:26](https://ref.ly/John24:26)

* **Associated Passages:** Luke 5:33; 1 Timothy 5:23; Acts 24:26

## πυλών (id: 004343000000000)

πυλών
=====

πυλών (G4440)

noun \| \-ῶνος \| πύλη

7\.48 · Constructions · Parts and Areas of Buildings

door, gate doors or gates used to close off entranceways πύλη\[a] and πυλών\[a] may refer to house doors and gates or to large doors and gates such as were used in a palace, temple, or in a city wall.  
Note, however, that in [ACT 14:13](https://ref.ly/John14:13) the reference may not be specifically to the gates as objects, but to the entranceway into the city.

gateway, entrance, vestibule the area associated with the entrance into a house or building The phrase τὰ πρὸς τὴν θύραν (see [7\.39](#7.39)) is essentially equivalent to πυλών\[b]: συνήχθησαν πολλοὶ ὥστε μηκέτι χωρεῖν μηδὲ τὰ πρὸς τὴν θύραν ‘so many people came together that there was no room left, not even out in the entrance’ [MRK 2:2](https://ref.ly/Matt2:2).

to inquire, to make an inquiry, to ask to inquire about something

[MAT 2:4](https://ref.ly/Matt2:4) · [LUK 15:26](https://ref.ly/Mark15:26) · [LUK 18:36](https://ref.ly/Mark18:36) · [JHN 4:52](https://ref.ly/Luke4:52) · [JHN 13:24](https://ref.ly/Luke13:24) · [ACT 4:7](https://ref.ly/John4:7) · [ACT 10:18](https://ref.ly/John10:18) · [ACT 10:29](https://ref.ly/John10:29) · [ACT 21:33](https://ref.ly/John21:33) · [ACT 23:19](https://ref.ly/John23:19)

27\.11 · Learn · Learn

to learn about, to find out about by inquiry to acquire information by questioning Since πυνθάνομαι\[b] and διερωτάω imply quite distinct semantic components, namely ‘to acquire information’ and ‘by the process of questioning,’ it may be appropriate in many languages to use two verbs meaning ‘to ask’ and ‘to find out.’ Accordingly, this expression in [ACT 10:17](https://ref.ly/John10:17) may be rendered as ‘meanwhile, men sent by Cornelius asked and learned where Simon’s house was.’

## πῦρ (id: 004345000000000)

πῦρ
===

πῦρ (G4442)

noun \| \-ός \| πυρόομαι

2\.3 · Natural Substances · Fire

fire the light, flames, and heat produced by the burning of materials Though the ancient view of the elements would lead to classification of this occurrence of πῦρ ([2PE 3:7](https://ref.ly/1Pet3:7)) as referring to a natural substance, the fact that the reference is to a particular event means that in many languages one must speak of a ‘burning,’ and this must be expressed frequently by a verb or participle rather than by a noun, for example, ‘for destruction by being burned up.’

8:7](https://ref.ly/Jude8:7) · [REV 8:8](https://ref.ly/Jude8:8) · [REV 9:17](https://ref.ly/Jude9:17) · [REV 9:18](https://ref.ly/Jude9:18) · [REV 10:1](https://ref.ly/Jude10:1) · [REV 11:5](https://ref.ly/Jude11:5) · [REV 13:13](https://ref.ly/Jude13:13) · [REV 14:10](https://ref.ly/Jude14:10) · [REV 14:18](https://ref.ly/Jude14:18) · [REV 15:2](https://ref.ly/Jude15:2) · [REV 16:8](https://ref.ly/Jude16:8) · [REV 17:16](https://ref.ly/Jude17:16) · [REV 18:8](https://ref.ly/Jude18:8) · [REV 19:12](https://ref.ly/Jude19:12) · [REV 19:20](https://ref.ly/Jude19:20) · [REV 20:9](https://ref.ly/Jude20:9) · [REV 20:14](https://ref.ly/Jude20:14) · [REV 20:14](https://ref.ly/Jude20:14) · [REV 20:15](https://ref.ly/Jude20:15) · [REV 21:8](https://ref.ly/Jude21:8)

2\.5 · Natural Substances · Fire

fire, bonfire a pile or heap of burning material The Greek phrase ἅψαντες ... πυράν ‘to kindle a fire’ is best rendered in a number of languages by a single verb meaning ‘to make a fire,’ and in some languages it is strange to speak of a ‘fire’ without indicating the type of material which is being burned. Hence, in [ACT 28:2](https://ref.ly/John28:2) the appropriate equivalent would be ‘they caused some sticks to burn.’  
In some languages a very important distinction is made between two functionally different kinds of fire, one used for getting rid of refuse and the other used for warming oneself. Fire as a destructive agent, mentioned frequently in the book of Revelation ([REV 8:5](https://ref.ly/Jude8:5) and [REV 20:10](https://ref.ly/Jude20:10)), may require quite a different term from what is used in speaking of a fire for the purpose of warming a person or heating an object.

[MAT 3:10](https://ref.ly/Matt3:10) · [MAT 7:19](https://ref.ly/Matt7:19) · [MAT 17:15](https://ref.ly/Matt17:15) · [LUK 3:9](https://ref.ly/Mark3:9) · [LUK 22:55](https://ref.ly/Mark22:55) · [JHN 15:6](https://ref.ly/Luke15:6) · [ACT 28:5](https://ref.ly/John28:5) · [REV 8:5](https://ref.ly/Jude8:5) · [REV 20:10](https://ref.ly/Jude20:10)

39\.15 · Hostility, Strife · Division

**Collocation:** πῦρ βάλλω

to cause discord, to cause division (an idiom, literally: to throw fire) to cause discord and contention Some translators have preferred to retain the literal rendering of this expression in [LUK 12:49](https://ref.ly/Mark12:49), since this seems to be necessary in view of the larger figurative context, which speaks of ‘how I wish it were already kindled’ (τί θέλω εἰ ἤδη ἀνήφθη). A strictly literal translation may, however, be understood in a completely physical sense, namely, ‘to set the world on fire.’ There are a number of quite diverse interpretations of the significance of this expression in [LUK 12:49](https://ref.ly/Mark12:49). Some have assumed, for example, that it refers to the fire of Pentecost.

[LUK 12:49](https://ref.ly/Mark12:49)

* **Associated Passages:** Matthew 3:11; Matthew 3:12; Matthew 5:22; Matthew 13:40; Matthew 13:42; Matthew 13:50; Matthew 18:8; Matthew 18:9; Matthew 25:41; Mark 9:22; Mark 9:43; Mark 9:48; Mark 9:49; Luke 3:16; Luke 3:17; Luke 9:54; Luke 17:29; Acts 2:3; Acts 2:19; Acts 7:30; Romans 12:20; 1 Corinthians 3:13; 1 Corinthians 3:15; Hebrews 1:7; Hebrews 10:27; Hebrews 11:34; Hebrews 12:18; Hebrews 12:29; James 3:5; James 3:6; James 5:3; 1 Peter 1:7; 2 Peter 3:7; Jude 1:7; Jude 1:23; Revelation 1:14; Revelation 2:18; Revelation 3:18; Revelation 4:5; Revelation 8:7; Revelation 8:8; Revelation 9:17; Revelation 9:18; Revelation 10:1; Revelation 11:5; Revelation 13:13; Revelation 14:10; Revelation 14:18; Revelation 15:2; Revelation 16:8; Revelation 17:16; Revelation 18:8; Revelation 19:12; Revelation 19:20; Revelation 20:9; Revelation 20:14; Revelation 20:15; Revelation 21:8; Matthew 3:10; Matthew 7:19; Matthew 17:15; Luke 3:9; Luke 22:55; John 15:6; Acts 28:5; Revelation 8:5; Revelation 20:10; Luke 12:49


## πυρά (id: 004346000000000)

πυρά
====

πυρά (G4443)

noun \| \-ᾶς \| πυρόομαι

2\.5 · Natural Substances · Fire

fire, bonfire a pile or heap of burning material The Greek phrase ἅψαντες ... πυράν ‘to kindle a fire’ is best rendered in a number of languages by a single verb meaning ‘to make a fire,’ and in some languages it is strange to speak of a ‘fire’ without indicating the type of material which is being burned. Hence, in [ACT 28:2](https://ref.ly/John28:2) the appropriate equivalent would be ‘they caused some sticks to burn.’  
In some languages a very important distinction is made between two functionally different kinds of fire, one used for getting rid of refuse and the other used for warming oneself. Fire as a destructive agent, mentioned frequently in the book of Revelation ([REV 8:5](https://ref.ly/Jude8:5) and [REV 20:10](https://ref.ly/Jude20:10)), may require quite a different term from what is used in speaking of a fire for the purpose of warming a person or heating an object.

## πύργος (id: 004347000000000)

πύργος
======

πύργος (G4444)

noun \| \-ου

7\.23 · Constructions · Buildings

tower, watchtower a tall structure with a lookout at the top; made of stone; for guarding a vineyard or a settlement In the NT πύργος may designate any type of tower, whether employed for military purposes or used by watchmen protecting a harvest.  
Some scholars have suggested that in [LUK 14:28](https://ref.ly/Mark14:28) the reference to πύργος is some kind of farm building, but this suggestion has been rejected by others.  
In a number of languages a word for ‘tower’ is simply ‘a high building’ (though of course not a skyscraper). In other instances a tower may be more appropriately described as ‘a high platform’ or ‘a high lookout place.’

[MAT 21:33](https://ref.ly/Matt21:33) · [MRK 12:1](https://ref.ly/Matt12:1) · [LUK 13:4](https://ref.ly/Mark13:4) · [LUK 14:28](https://ref.ly/Mark14:28)

* **Associated Passages:** Matthew 21:33; Mark 12:1; Luke 13:4; Luke 14:28


## πυρέσσω (id: 004348000000000)

πυρέσσω
=======

πυρέσσω (G4445)

πυρόομαι

23\.159 · Physiological Processes and States · Sickness, Disease, Weakness

to have a fever, fever to be sick with a fever Though in a number of languages one can say ‘a person has a fever,’ in some languages the reverse expression is used, for example, ‘the fever has a person.’ Similarly, instead of saying ‘a person has a disease,’ it is ‘the disease which grabs a person.’ One may often speak of a fever as ‘to become hot’ or ‘to burn’ or ‘to be sick with burning.’

[MAT 8:14](https://ref.ly/Matt8:14) · [MRK 1:30](https://ref.ly/Matt1:30)

## πυρόω (id: 004351000000000)

πυρόω
=====

πυρόω (G4448) — πυρόομαι

ἀναζωπυρέω · πῦρ · πυρά · πυρέσσω · πυρετός · πύρινος · πυρράζω · πυρρός · πύρωσις

79\.72 · Features of Objects · Hot, Lukewarm, Cold

to heat, to make fiery hot to cause to be hot, as of fire

[REV 1:15](https://ref.ly/Jude1:15)

14\.63 · (πυρόομαι\[a]) · Physical Events and States · Burning

to burn, burning, to be on fire the process of burning In a number of languages important distinctions are made in the use of terms for burning. For example, the burning of a substance for light, as in the case of a lamp or candle, may be indicated by a term quite different from an expression indicating the destruction or burning up of something. Similarly, the burning of wood for cooking may require still another term. The extent or size of the fire involved may also require some distinctions in terms for burning.  
In a number of languages it is extremely difficult to indicate burning as means simply by a phrase such as ‘by burning.’ Normally, burning can only be referred to by means of an expression referring to fire. Accordingly, [2PE 3:12](https://ref.ly/1Pet3:12) may be rendered as ‘a great fire will destroy the sky and will cause the elements to melt’; likewise, in [ACT 28:2](https://ref.ly/John28:2) one may translate ‘in the end a great fire will destroy the earth.’

[EPH 6:16](https://ref.ly/Gal6:16) · [2PE 3:12](https://ref.ly/1Pet3:12) · [REV 3:18](https://ref.ly/Jude3:18)

25\.229 · (πυρόομαι\[b]) · Attitudes and Emotions · Worry, Anxiety, Distress, Peace

to be greatly worried, to be very distressed, to be worried and distressed (a figurative extension of meaning of πυρόω\[b] ‘to burn,’ [14\.63](#14.63)) to be upset, with great concern and anxiety

[2CO 11:29](https://ref.ly/1Cor11:29)

25\.31 · (πυρόομαι\[c]) · Attitudes and Emotions · Desire Strongly

to burn with passion, to be sexually aroused to experience intense sexual desire

[1CO 7:9](https://ref.ly/Rom7:9)

* **Associated Passages:** Revelation 1:15; Ephesians 6:16; 2 Peter 3:12; Revelation 3:18; 2 Corinthians 11:29; 1 Corinthians 7:9

adj \| \-ά, \-όν \| πυρόομαι

fiery red fiery red (probably with a tinge of yellow or orange) As in other instances of color terms, it may be necessary in [REV 6:4](https://ref.ly/Jude6:4) to use an expression which is particularly applicable to a horse.

## πύρωσις (id: 004355000000000)

πύρωσις
=======

πύρωσις (G4451)

noun \| \-εως \| πυρόομαι

14\.63 · Physical Events and States · Burning

to burn, burning, to be on fire the process of burning In a number of languages important distinctions are made in the use of terms for burning. For example, the burning of a substance for light, as in the case of a lamp or candle, may be indicated by a term quite different from an expression indicating the destruction or burning up of something. Similarly, the burning of wood for cooking may require still another term. The extent or size of the fire involved may also require some distinctions in terms for burning.  
It is important to indicate that in the phrase ‘her burning’ she is the one who is burned. One may therefore render this part of [REV 18:18](https://ref.ly/Jude18:18) as ‘when they see the smoke of the fire that burns her up.’

[REV 18:9](https://ref.ly/Jude18:9) · [REV 18:18](https://ref.ly/Jude18:18)

24\.91 · Sensory Events and States · Pain, Suffering

to suffer pain, to experience severe suffering, painful suffering (a figurative extension of meaning of πύρωσις\[a] ‘to burn,’ [14\.63](#14.63)) the experience of painful suffering For another interpretation of πύρωσις in [1PE 4:12](https://ref.ly/Jas4:12), see [78\.37](#78.37).

[1PE 4:12](https://ref.ly/Jas4:12)

78\.37 · Degree · More Than, Less Than \[Comparative Degree]

intense, intensity, exceptional degree of (a figurative extension of meaning of πύρωσις\[a] ‘burning,’ [14\.63](#14.63)) an exceptionally intense degree of some experience, with the implication of suffering For another interpretation of πύρωσις in [1PE 4:12](https://ref.ly/Jas4:12), see [24\.91](#24.91).

[1PE 4:12](https://ref.ly/Jas4:12)

* **Associated Passages:** Revelation 18:9; Revelation 18:18; 1 Peter 4:12


## πωλέω (id: 004356000000000)

πωλέω
=====

πωλέω (G4453)

πορφυρόπωλις

57\.186 · Possess, Transfer, Exchange · Sell, Buy, Price

to sell to dispose of property or provide services in exchange for money or other valuable considerations πιπράσκω and ἀποδίδομαι (ἀποδίδωμι\[e]) may also be used to refer to selling a person into slavery. Note, for example, πιπράσκω in [MAT 18:25](https://ref.ly/Matt18:25) (ἐκέλευσεν αὐτὸν ὁ κύριος πραθῆναι ‘his master ordered him to be sold as a slave’) and ἀποδίδομαι in [ACT 7:9](https://ref.ly/John7:9) (οἱ πατριάρχαι ζηλώσαντες τὸν Ἰωσὴφ ἀπέδοντο εἰς Αἴγυπτον ‘the patriarchs were jealous of Joseph and sold him to be a slave in Egypt’).  
In a number of languages, however, it may be important to use a distinctive term for selling a person into slavery. In fact, it may be necessary to describe the event rather explicitly, for example, ‘to receive money for handing a person over to someone else to be a slave.’  
In [ROM 7:14](https://ref.ly/Acts7:14) πιπράσκω is used figuratively in the phrase πεπραμένος ὑπὸ τὴν ἁμαρτίαν ‘sold as a slave to sin,’ in the sense that sin is personified as the master.

[MAT 10:29](https://ref.ly/Matt10:29) · [MAT 13:44](https://ref.ly/Matt13:44) · [MAT 19:21](https://ref.ly/Matt19:21) · [MAT 21:12](https://ref.ly/Matt21:12) · [MAT 21:12](https://ref.ly/Matt21:12) · [MAT 25:9](https://ref.ly/Matt25:9) · [MRK 10:21](https://ref.ly/Matt10:21) · [MRK 11:15](https://ref.ly/Matt11:15) · [MRK 11:15](https://ref.ly/Matt11:15) · [LUK 12:6](https://ref.ly/Mark12:6) · [LUK 12:33](https://ref.ly/Mark12:33) · [LUK 17:28](https://ref.ly/Mark17:28) · [LUK 18:22](https://ref.ly/Mark18:22) · [LUK 19:45](https://ref.ly/Mark19:45) · [LUK 22:36](https://ref.ly/Mark22:36) · [JHN 2:14](https://ref.ly/Luke2:14) · [JHN 2:16](https://ref.ly/Luke2:16) · [ACT 4:34](https://ref.ly/John4:34) · [ACT 4:37](https://ref.ly/John4:37) · [ACT 5:1](https://ref.ly/John5:1) · [1CO 10:25](https://ref.ly/Rom10:25) · [REV 13:17](https://ref.ly/Jude13:17)
